content:
Goal: Create a surreal industrial Renaissance collage poster titled {argument name="headline text" default="MASS PRODUCED AUTHENTICITY"}, mixing classical sculpture, factory machinery, architectural blueprints, and obsolete computer error imagery into a critical art-print composition.

Canvas: Square poster, 1:1 aspect ratio, aged beige paper background with stains, faint grid lines, registration marks, technical drafting annotations, and a worn archival print texture. Use a muted palette of stone gray, dusty beige, charcoal black, rust orange, faded red, and small accents of Windows-95 blue.

Layout: The upper left contains the main typography: small technical labels reading “SERIES: MPA_001” and “DATE: 05.19.95”, then large elegant serif words “MASS PRODUCED” in dark gray, with the word “AUTHENTICITY” below in tall condensed distressed red-orange type. The left side also includes a small schematic caption block reading “THE ASSEMBLY LINE OF IDENTITY STANDARDIZED, REPLICATED, EMPTY.” followed by “FIG. 07A” and “DAVID PRODUCTION UNIT SCHEMATIC”. The right upper quadrant contains a detailed circular engineering blueprint diagram with measurements and the labels “SECTION A-A” and “SCALE 1:10”. The bottom left contains a small conveyor-belt plan drawing labeled like a technical manufacturing diagram.

Main scene: Across the lower half, show a long conveyor belt receding from left to right, carrying a row of identical classical marble bust heads inspired by Michelangelo’s David. There are exactly 10 visible bust heads arranged along the conveyor, all pale stone with curly Renaissance hair, repeated like factory products. Cover the front faces with exactly 7 opaque gray rectangular censorship blocks of different sizes, aligned over the bust faces: one small block at the far left, two medium blocks left-of-center, one very large vertical block in the center foreground, two medium blocks on the right, and one smaller block near the far right.

Central object: Behind the central bust, a huge pixelated white computer mouse cursor arrow rises diagonally upward, built like a heavy stone or concrete 3D object with dark beveled edges. It is crashing through cracked plaster and rubble, exposing tangled wires, circuit boards, metal components, and broken concrete beneath. Add flying debris and fracture lines around the impact point.

Foreground details: In the lower right foreground, place a rusty red industrial machine housing with chipped paint and black mechanical parts. Overlay a small retro Windows error dialog box tilted slightly, with a blue title bar reading “Error”, a red X icon, the message “Authenticity.exe has stopped working.”, and an “OK” button. On the rusty machine, add black stamped text reading “ERROR”, “CODE: 0x000404”, and “SYSTEM_FAILURE”. Include dark cables, gears, bolts, grime, and orange rust stains spilling into the bottom area.

Visual style: Surreal 404 Renaissance collage, brutalist industrial assemblage, archival blueprint poster, photomontage mixed with engraved technical drawing, distressed printmaking, high-detail textures, cracked stone, exposed electronics, analog paper grain, subtle halftone, museum-conservation beige background, cinematic yet flat editorial composition.

Constraints: Keep all visible text legible and integrated into the print. Use exactly 10 bust heads and exactly 7 gray censorship rectangles. Do not add extra characters, logos, watermarks, modern UI beyond the single retro error dialog, or bright saturated colors outside the small blue error title bar and rust-red typography/machinery.

content:
Goal: Create a surreal Renaissance-style 404 collage poster about digital memory loss, combining ancient architecture, retro computing, anatomical diagrams, and distressed editorial typography.

Canvas: Square 1:1 composition, aged beige paper background with torn-paper collage edges, halftone stains, fine grid lines, blueprint registration marks, and archival print texture. Use a muted palette of warm parchment beige, charcoal black, concrete gray, rusty red, and faded blue.

Main subject: A monumental classical temple resembling the Parthenon dominates the center and lower half, shown in three-quarter perspective. It has a long colonnade with exactly 16 visible white stone columns. The structure is cracked open and partially collapsed, revealing dense server racks, circuit boards, cables, and exposed wiring inside the ruins. Large chunks of plaster and stone are missing, with jagged torn edges.

Key surreal object: A giant black 3.5-inch floppy disk crashes into the right foreground at a diagonal angle, half buried in shattered concrete, sending a dramatic burst of debris outward. The floppy disk has a pale rectangular label area and visible side ridges.

Text and typography: On the upper left, set a large editorial serif headline reading “{argument name="headline text" default="MEMORIES SAVED, SOULS DELETED"}”, with “SAVED,” and “DELETED” in rusty red and the rest in dark charcoal. Above it, add small technical text reading “FIG. 01” and “HUMAN MEMORY ARCHITECTURE (FRAGMENTED)”. Beneath the headline, add small typewriter-style lines reading “THE PAST IS PRESERVED. THE SELF IS LOST.” and “ARCHIVE PROTOCOL_404 VERSION: 7.1.9”. Keep the typography elegant, old scientific, and slightly misregistered.

Diagram details: Include exactly 2 anatomical brain illustrations on the upper right: one large floating brain labeled like a scientific plate, and one smaller circular cross-section brain diagram below it. Add exactly 3 circular technical diagrams around the poster: one large faint circle near the top center-left, one central crosshair circle labeled “BRAIN SECTION_017”, and one small left-side circle near the columns. Add thin measuring lines, tiny numerals, plus signs, and red calibration marks throughout.

Foreground detail: Place exactly 1 small retro Windows-style error dialog near the bottom center. It should have a blue title bar reading “System Error”, a red X error icon, body text reading “Critical Failure. Memory cannot be saved. Continue?”, and a single button labeled “OK”.

Style: Hyper-detailed photomontage, surreal 404 error Renaissance collage, archaeological ruin meets cybernetic archive, analog computer nostalgia, brutalist marble dust, museum plate layout, screenprint grain, torn paper layers, precise but distressed graphic design. No people, no modern clean UI except the single error dialog, no extra headline text, no watermark.
title: Surreal 404 Renaissance Memory Collage
description: Generates a distressed surreal collage poster of a ruined classical temple merged with servers, a floppy disk crash, brain diagrams, and retro system-error typography.

content:
Goal: Create a 3:2 horizontal surreal Renaissance-collage poster about a digital 404 void, combining antique manuscript design, classical sculpture, cybernetic ruin, and obsolete computer UI.

Canvas: Square-ish 3:2 editorial poster composition on aged beige parchment paper with stains, fibers, faint grid lines, registration marks, thin drafting guides, tiny technical annotations, and a torn-paper lower edge revealing a strip of dark glitchy code/static underneath.

Main subject: A colossal reclining Renaissance marble statue of a bearded male figure, like a fallen classical god or philosopher, filling the lower two-thirds of the image diagonally from bottom left to upper right. The stone body is cracked, eroded, and broken open in multiple places, exposing dense gold-and-black circuitry, server boards, mechanical ribs, and electronic components inside. Thick black and gray cables coil around and through the statue, wrapping the torso, shoulder, arm, and legs like vines. Add heavy industrial metal braces and rails strapped across the figure, with small triangular markings and embedded panels. The statue’s head is tilted back toward the upper right, eyes closed or lifeless, with a weathered curly beard and hair.

Background and symbols: Place one large muted burnt-orange red sun disk behind the statue’s head and upper torso, slightly right of center. Scatter exactly 44 small black hourglass icons across the upper half of the poster, varying size and opacity, with a few larger hourglass symbols near the top right and upper center. Behind the figure, include a distant broken stone wall or ruined fortress silhouette running horizontally.

Typography and left panel: On the upper left, set a large refined serif headline in tall condensed Renaissance editorial type: “SCROLLING INTO THE VOID”, stacked in four lines, with “VOID” in muted rusty red and the other words in dark charcoal. Add tiny technical labels around it such as “FIG. 7”, “MEMENTO MORI”, “SYSTEM FAILURE”, and faint Latin-like filler text. Below the headline, create a thin vertical index with exactly 3 numbered entries labeled 01, 02, and 03, each with tiny unreadable pseudo-academic notes. Add delicate compass, measurement, and chart lines in the left and center background.

Error UI detail: Near the lower right over the cables and statue, place one small retro Windows-style dialog box with a blue title bar reading “System Error”. The dialog message should say: “The universe has encountered a fatal error. Reality cannot continue.” Include exactly one centered gray button labeled “OK”.

Visual style: Hyper-detailed surreal collage, neo-Renaissance engraving meets cyberpunk archaeology, photorealistic textures mixed with vintage print design, muted sepia palette with charcoal black, stone gray, oxidized gold circuitry, and a single rusty red accent. Use dramatic but soft museum-like lighting, crisp detail, layered paper textures, and high-end editorial poster composition.

Constraints: Keep the composition spacious and horizontal with strong negative space in the upper left; no modern glossy sci-fi neon except subtle glitch colors at the torn bottom edge; no extra people; no watermark; preserve the exact main headline text {argument name="headline text" default="SCROLLING INTO THE VOID"}, the red sun color {argument name="sun color" default="muted burnt-orange red"}, the statue subject {argument name="statue subject" default="reclining bearded Renaissance marble god"}, the error message {argument name="error message" default="The universe has encountered a fatal error. Reality cannot continue."}, and the poster material {argument name="poster material" default="aged beige parchment paper"}.
title: Surreal 404 Renaissance Collage
description: Generates a horizontal surreal editorial poster of a broken Renaissance statue merged with circuitry, antique typography, hourglasses, and a retro system error.

content:
Goal: Create a 3×3 Japanese meme-style process chart showing how an original character request mutates through a production pipeline, using the same turquoise-haired fantasy belly dancer/queen character across all panels. Make it humorous, polished, and information-dense, like a comparison sheet or comic grid.

Canvas: Vertical 3:4 image with nine equal rectangular panels separated by thick black borders. Each panel has a white title strip at the top with bold black Japanese text and a circled number. Use a clean manga/illustration presentation with consistent margins.

Panel count and labels: Include exactly 9 panels, arranged left to right, top to bottom:
1. 「① クライアントが説明したキャラ設定」 — a childlike crayon sketch of the character, simple round face, long aqua hair, veil, gold jewelry, white dress, blue gem ornaments, uneven hand-drawn lines on paper.
2. 「② プロデューサーの理解」 — a refined but still relatively modest anime concept portrait of the turquoise-haired veiled dancer in a white-and-gold jeweled outfit, warm indoor lighting.
3. 「③ ディレクターの解釈」 — an exaggerated glamorous fantasy illustration with dramatic pose, sparkling purple cosmic background, lots of gold chains and turquoise gems, more sensual and overproduced.
4. 「④ クライアントへの説明・約束」 — a beautiful polished sales-pitch version, elegant magical lighting, butterflies or glowing particles, romantic pastel sparkle atmosphere, ornate jewelry and flowing veil.
5. 「⑤ 作られた設定資料」 — a professional character design sheet with exactly 2 full-body views of the character, front and back/side, plus multiple accessory diagrams of necklaces, chains, earrings, and turquoise-gold ornaments on a white background with small sketch notes.
6. 「⑥ イラストレーターのラフ」 — a monochrome rough pencil sketch of the character, energetic loose linework, long flowing hair and veil, jewelry indicated with scribbly detail, unfinished but dynamic.
7. 「⑦ 実際の成果物」 — make this an illustrated comic-book poster, not a 3D figure: bold saturated colors, thick ink outlines, pop-art halftone texture, the character transformed into a flashy “EXOTIC QUEEN!” cover-style pin-up with large turquoise hair, gold coins and jewelry, yellow-pink background, text reading “EXOTIC QUEEN!” in the upper left.
8. 「⑧ クライアントへの請求金額」 — a lavish invoice scene with a parchment invoice reading 「御請求書」 and the large price “¥3,980,000”, gold coins, gems, a royal seal, and a tiny chibi version of the turquoise-haired character near the bottom.
9. 「⑨ 本当にクライアントが欲しかったキャラ」 — the final desired character: elegant, mysterious, modestly sensual veiled fantasy queen in soft blue fabric, glowing lanterns, warm bokeh, delicate turquoise gems and gold chains, graceful pose and cinematic atmosphere.

Character details: The recurring character is {argument name="character concept" default="a turquoise-haired veiled exotic fantasy queen with gold jewelry and blue gemstones"}. She has very long aqua/turquoise hair, a translucent veil, ornate gold headpieces, turquoise gemstone accessories, layered necklaces, armlets, waist chains, and flowing pale blue or white fabric. Keep the same overall identity across all panels while changing the art style and interpretation per panel.

Text customization: Use the Japanese title labels exactly as listed. The invoice amount should be {argument name="invoice amount" default="¥3,980,000"}. The pop-art poster phrase should be {argument name="poster phrase" default="EXOTIC QUEEN!"}.

Visual style: High-resolution anime illustration collage, humorous production-process infographic, crisp panel borders, varied rendering styles per panel: crayon sketch, polished anime, fantasy splash art, design sheet, rough pencil, pop-art comic, invoice prop scene, and cinematic final concept art. Add abundant sparkle, gold, turquoise, and ornate decorative motifs.

Constraints: Use exactly 9 panels and exactly the 9 listed panel labels. Keep each panel visually distinct. Make panel 7 clearly a flat illustration/comic poster rather than a figurine or 3D object. Do not add extra panels, extra captions, watermarks, or logos.
title: Nine-Panel Character Production Meme
description: Generates a Japanese 3×3 comic-style process chart showing how a fantasy character concept changes through client, producer, director, illustrator, invoice, and final desired versions.

content: 2x2 grid, 16:9, do this for 4 famous philosophies: class Philosophical_Idea_Machine_DNA:     def __init__(self):         self.subject = "{argument name="subject" default="[PHILOSOPHY / CONCEPT / PARADOX]"}"          self.parents = {             "composition_parent": "Renaissance memory theatre — wheels within wheels, concentric knowledge rings, symbolic emblems",             "atmosphere_parent": "Alchemical manuscript — cryptic imagery, lunar light, sepia and oxidized copper tones",             "graphic_parent": "Bauhaus conceptualism — elemental shapes as thought-forms, clean lines, primary colour logic",             "detail_parent": "Encyclopédie plate — mechanical cross-sections of ideas, gears of dialectic, part numbering"         }          self.mutations = {             "medium_mutation": "Printed layers of translucent vellum overlays that build the idea when viewed together",             "information_mutation": "Logical syllogisms as circuit diagrams, historical proponents as constellation maps, glossary of terms",             "semantic_mutation": "The abstract idea becomes a physical, inhabitable clockwork universe",             "scale_mutation": "A single logical step unfolds into a complete microcosmic city where the idea is lived reality"         }          self. style_mix = {             "composition_parent": 0.25,             "atmosphere_parent": 0.20,             "graphic_parent": 0.30,             "detail_parent": 0.15,             "mutations": 0.10         }              def generate_subject(self):         subject = """         Create a vertical luxury infographic poster that gives physical form to [SUBJECT].          Invent a magnificent symbolic machine, diagram, or architecture that represents the idea.         Explode its layers to show internal logic, historical influences, counterarguments,         and real-world manifestations. The design must feel like a transmission from         a library that exists outside time.          Semantic inference should render the intangible tangible with intellectual rigour.         """                  return render(             subject,             style_mix=self. style_mix,             format="vertical poster, conceptual infographic",             typography="""             Large title: {argument name="concept name" default="[CONCEPT NAME]"}             Subtitle: [ORIGIN / PHILOSOPHER / MAXIM]             Microtext: [LOGICAL FORMULA / CITATION / FIRST USE DATE / LATIN TERM]             """,             composition="""             Mandala-like symmetry or dynamic tension depending on concept nature.             Clear visual hierarchy, meditative spacing.             """,             lighting="""             Illumination from unseen source — like a page from a sacred text held to candlelight.             """,             constraints="""             No trite symbols (lightbulbs for ideas, etc.). No pseudo-profound emptiness.             """         )
title: Philosophical Infographic Machine Generator

content:

Canvas: 1024×768 landscape screenshot with a dark charcoal modal editor centered on screen, rounded corners, subtle shadow, and a faint glimpse of the underlying slide behind the modal edges. The interface zoom indicator at the top center reads “42%”.

Layout: At the top left, show the Japanese title “画像の編集” with two small undo/redo arrow icons. At the top right, show a small connected-dots icon and an X close icon. Below the top bar, center a floating toolbar with exactly 6 tools labeled: “補正”, “トリミング”, “背景”, “領域の選択”, “テキスト”, plus their small simple line icons, arranged horizontally in dark rounded rectangles.

Main editing area: In the center, place a square image area outlined with a thin purple border. Inside it, use a black and dark-gray checkerboard transparency pattern. Near the top of this square, show a dark notification toast with a green check icon and the Japanese text “背景が削除されました。” plus a small X close icon.

Subject details: Center the icon inside the checkerboard square: one pastel yume-kawaii white rabbit mascot with a thick pale pink outline, oversized rounded head, two long upright ears with pink inner ears, small brown glossy eyes, pink oval blush cheeks, and a happy open mouth. The bunny holds exactly 1 mint-aqua heart-shaped object in front of its body with two rounded paws visible. Add soft highlights on the heart and a clean sticker-like vector style with no harsh shadows. The character should feel like a cute app icon or sticker. Use {argument name="character" default="a white kawaii rabbit"}, {argument name="held object" default="a mint-aqua heart"}, and {argument name="accent color" default="pale pink"}.

Footer and controls: At the bottom center of the modal, show the small Japanese warning text “AI 生成コンテンツは間違っている可能性があります。” At the bottom right, show exactly 2 buttons: an orange “保存” button and a dark gray “キャンセル” button. Near the right side of the image area, include exactly 2 small vertical feedback icons: thumbs up above thumbs down.

Visual style: Realistic software screenshot, crisp UI text, dark Microsoft-style interface, clean Japanese typography, transparent-background checkerboard, cute pastel sticker art. Keep the composition faithful to a PowerPoint built-in image editor after background removal, not a standalone illustration.

content:
Goal: Create a square educational fashion reference poster showing {argument name="theme" default="Han dynasty women's clothing in different forms"}, with eight photorealistic full-body female models wearing traditional Chinese Hanfu. Faces should be softly blurred or left featureless for privacy, while clothing, hairstyles, and accessories are highly detailed.

Canvas: Square 1:1 poster, clean off-white background with thin gutters between panels. At the top center, add the Chinese title {argument name="headline text" default="汉朝女子服饰  不同形制展示"} in elegant dark brown calligraphy-style serif lettering, with small decorative flourishes on both sides.

Layout: Arrange exactly 8 vertical image panels in a 4 columns by 2 rows grid. Each panel contains one standing woman from about knees to head, centered, hands folded or gently holding sleeves, with a shallow-depth-of-field traditional courtyard or palace background. Each panel has a small vertical cream paper label near the upper left or upper right with black Chinese calligraphy, plus a short Chinese description in a translucent white strip along the bottom.

Eight panels, counted and labeled exactly:
1. 「曲裾深衣」 — white layered robe with wrapped curved front, pale peach sash, very wide sleeves, ornate floral updo, pink blossom courtyard background.
2. 「直裾深衣」 — rust-orange outer robe over cream inner layers, embroidered lapels, wide belt, long sleeve drape, dark wooden interior background.

content:
Goal: Create a cinematic vertical historical figure archive poster about {argument name="character name" default="班超"}, showing a heroic ancient Chinese frontier general breaking out of a documentary-style infographic layout.

Canvas: Vertical 3:4 poster, 768 x 1024 feel, sepia-gold and smoky gray historical palette, dramatic backlight, dust, battlefield haze, high-detail 3D realism mixed with museum exhibition graphics.

Main subject: One full-body ancient Chinese armored commander stands in the center foreground, low-angle heroic composition. He wears layered dark lamellar armor, engraved belt plates, leather boots, red-brown cloak blowing leftward, and a stern dignified expression. His left arm thrusts forward toward the viewer holding a tall wooden command tablet engraved with Chinese calligraphy; his right hand holds a vertical staff or ceremonial standard with a tassel and Chinese characters. Make the figure feel three-dimensional, as if stepping out of the flat poster frame.

Background: Ancient Western Regions frontier setting with rocky desert ground, mountains in the distance, mounted soldiers and caravan silhouettes on the left, and a large fortified city gate on the right with banners, scaffolding, guards, and the gate plaque reading in Chinese. Add a large weathered war banner in the upper right. The atmosphere should suggest expedition, diplomacy, military command, and westward expansion.

Left typography block: Place very large black brush-calligraphy title text {argument name="main title text" default="班超"} vertically on the upper left. Beneath it, add smaller vertical Chinese biographical text describing birthplace, role, and lifespan, including the years 32—102. Add a small red seal stamp below the biographical block.

Floating archive cards: Add exactly 8 translucent glass-like historical event cards around the character, each with thin gold borders, tiny corner rivets, Chinese text, and monochrome documentary mini-illustrations. Count and place them as follows: 1) top center card, labeled 01 and 73, about joining the army after abandoning the brush; 2) left-middle card, labeled 02 and 73, about mission to the Western Regions; 3) top right card, labeled 03 and 91, about pacifying the northern Xiongnu; 4) right-middle card, labeled 04 and 95, about suppressing rebellion; 5) lower-left-middle card, labeled 05 and 96, about establishing the Protectorate of the Western Regions; 6) bottom-left card, labeled 06, about governing for forty years and uniting many states; 7) lower-right-middle card, labeled 07, about opening the Silk Road; 8) bottom-right card, labeled 08 and 102, about returning to Luoyang in old age. Each card should include a small faded illustration of cavalry, officials, soldiers, maps, or caravans.

Bottom timeline: Add exactly 6 timeline milestones along the bottom edge with a thin gold horizontal line, small dots, and Chinese labels: 32 birth, 73 joining army and mission west, 91 northern Xiongnu, 95 suppressing rebellion, 96 setting up the Protectorate, 102 successful return to Luoyang. Keep the timeline legible but secondary.

Visual style: Epic Chinese historical 3D poster, ultra-detailed armor textures, cinematic volumetric lighting, dust particles, depth of field, parchment-like overlays, museum archive infographic design, gold linework, realistic fabric motion, dramatic perspective.

Constraints: Use exactly one central historical figure, exactly 8 floating event cards, and exactly 6 bottom timeline milestones. Keep all visible text in Chinese calligraphy or Chinese serif style, with no English labels in the poster. Avoid modern objects, neon colors, cartoons, watermarks, or extra characters dominating the central figure.

content:

Use an epic museum-exhibition style with realistic 3D rendering, documentary historical atmosphere, and floating translucent data panels.

Canvas: Vertical 3:4 poster, warm sepia and dusty gold color palette, high detail, dramatic backlight, hazy sky, windblown sand, ancient city silhouettes and pagodas in the distance. The central figure occupies most of the height, slightly right of center, walking forward with strong depth and scale.

Main subject: A bald Tang dynasty Buddhist monk in layered weathered gray-brown robes, wrapped sashes, prayer beads around the neck, worn cloth textures, dust and wind movement. He holds a tall ornate monk staff in his right hand and extends an ancient rolled scripture tube or sutra scroll case toward the viewer in his left hand, creating a strong foreground perspective. The face area should be softly obscured or neutral rather than a recognizable modern face. The pose should feel determined, solemn, and pioneering.

Left title column: Large expressive black ink-brush Chinese calligraphy reading {argument name="main calligraphy title" default="玄奘"} at the upper left. Beside it, add thin vertical Chinese-style biographical text indicating Tang dynasty monk, westward scripture journey, Buddhist transmission, and lifespan 602—664. Add a small red seal stamp under the biographical text. Add an additional slim vertical annotation line near the lower left like an archival quote or route note.

Floating archive panels: Include exactly 6 semi-transparent glass panels with thin gold outlines, small node connectors, white/gold text, faint historical illustration backgrounds, and a numbered sequence. The six panels are: 01, year 629, departure from Chang'an; 02, year 630, crossing Gaochang; 03, year 635, reaching Tianzhu/India; 04, year 645, studying Buddhism and returning east; 05, year 653, collecting and bringing back scriptures; 06, years 645—664, translating classics and transmitting them to later generations. Place panels around the figure: 01 upper left, 02 mid-left, 03 upper right, 04 right middle, 05 lower right, 06 lower left. Keep the panels legible but ethereal, as if holographic museum labels.

Bottom timeline: Add one thin horizontal timeline across the bottom with exactly 6 marked dots labeled 629, 630, 635, 645, 653, and 664. Under the dots, add short Chinese-style event captions matching the six archive panels. Use small refined serif or Song-style typography in off-white/gold.

Background details: A vast desert road with caravan silhouettes, camels, monks, travelers, dust clouds, distant mountains, and ancient frontier architecture. Include faint ruined city walls and pagoda-like structures to support the historical westward journey atmosphere. Make the scroll case and monk staff break the flat poster plane visually, as if protruding from the archival layout.

Visual style: {argument name="visual style" default="ultra-realistic 3D historical poster, cinematic lighting, museum archive infographic, sepia desert palette, volumetric dust, high-detail cloth and metal textures"}. Elegant, solemn, heroic, not cartoonish. Combine realistic historical portraiture with infographic overlays and Chinese ink-calligraphy aesthetics.

Constraints: Use exactly 1 central character, exactly 6 floating archive panels, and exactly 6 bottom timeline markers. No modern objects, no English text on the poster unless requested, no watermark, no logo, no extra characters in the foreground.

He wears layered worn robes, leather belts, travel wraps, boots, and a red-brown scarf blowing in the wind. His left arm stretches forward holding a large unrolled parchment map that projects toward the viewer in forced perspective; the map is labeled in Chinese as {argument name="map title" default="西域线路图"} and contains route lines and place names. His right hand holds a tall ceremonial staff with an ornate metal finial and a hanging vertical red banner reading {argument name="banner text" default="汉节"}. Behind him, show a caravan of camels, riders, soldiers, flags, and a distant western-region city gate under a hazy sky with birds.

Add smaller vertical Chinese descriptors beside it describing him as an opener of the Western Regions and Silk Road pioneer, plus dates “前164—前114” and a small red seal stamp. His right arm thrusts a long rolled nautical map directly toward the viewer, creating strong foreshortening. His left hand rests near the ship rail. The ship cuts through rough waves, sea spray, and mist. A distant harbor city and fleet silhouettes appear low on the left horizon, with a few birds in the cloudy sky.

Add the date range “1371—1433” and a small red seal stamp. Include a narrow vertical note about the Ming dynasty navigator and diplomat opening the maritime Silk Road.

content:
Goal: Create a dramatic Japanese promotional infographic poster for a highly opinionated tonkatsu specialty restaurant, {argument name="restaurant name" default="かつ人 (Katsujin)"}, focused on the rule that miso katsu sauce is dip-only and double-dipping is forbidden.

Canvas: Wide horizontal 16:9 poster divided into three vertical panels by thin antique-gold borders. Use a black charcoal background with smoky texture, dark red brush accents, warm gold typography, and premium izakaya/ramen-shop menu aesthetics. Overall mood: intense, masculine, theatrical, slightly satirical.

Left panel: Show a realistic close-up food photo style scene: a black ceramic plate with sliced golden-brown tonkatsu, one piece held by black chopsticks and dipped into a small bowl of dark miso sauce with sesame seeds. Include shredded cabbage piled high and a bowl of rice in the background. At the top, large Japanese headline text: 「味噌カツはディップ限定」. Under it, a red paint-stroke banner with big white text: 「二度漬け禁止」. Add a subtitle: 「これが『かつ人(Katsujin)』流。」. At the bottom, place a framed information box titled 「かつ人（Katsujin）ディップのこだわり」 with exactly 5 checkmark bullet points: 「味噌かつはディップ限定」, 「味噌ダレは必ず小鉢提供」, 「サクサク衣を守る」, 「食べる量は自分で決める」, 「二度漬け禁止」. Include a circular gold brush logo reading 「かつ人 KATSUJIN」 and a parchment note: 「『ソースはディップ限定』という決まりさえ守れば、他は基本自由。うまさの選択肢を守る。それが、かつ人（Katsujin）のこだわりです。」

Center panel: Create a text-heavy editorial explanation column. Header: 「かつ人（Katsujin）ディップのこだわり」. Add multiple short Japanese paragraphs explaining the origin of the dip rule, with key emphasis lines in red. Include a boxed comparison strip with exactly 3 labeled sauce choices: 「特製ソース」, 「味噌かつ」, 「ウスターソース」. Beneath it, add a bold central rule line: 「全てディップ。全て二度漬け禁止。」. Continue with dense explanatory Japanese copy about preserving crisp breading, choosing sauce quantity, and respecting restaurant etiquette. End with a framed bottom warning emphasizing: 「唯一の決まりは『味噌かつはディップ限定』。」 Keep the text visually plausible and neatly typeset, but prioritize legibility of major headings and rules.

Right panel: Top section shows a chibi-style stern chef mascot with short brown hair, red glasses, small mustache, suit-like chef outfit, and chopsticks held over a pot of sauce, with steam rising. Header text above: 「パスタを二つ折りにする石人」. Below the mascot, add a framed warning box: 「本場イタリアではパスタを二つ折りにする行為は『子供に食べさせるパスタ』と卑下されています。」. Middle section shows a dramatic manga-style angry man pointing directly at the viewer, black suit, fiery red smoky background, intense expression; place a simple rectangular censor blur over the center of the face. Add large vertical red brush text beside him: 「ディップ以外は邪道だ」 and a smaller title above: 「範馬勇次郎風の石人」. Bottom section contains another framed quote box: 「上等な料理に、ハチミツをぶちまけるが如し思想！！と非難されても仕方ないのです。」. Finish with a large gold calligraphy-style logo at the bottom: 「かつ人」 with small roman letters 「KATSUJIN」.

Visual style: Photorealistic food on the left, dense Japanese magazine-ad typography in the center, anime/chibi and manga caricature elements on the right. Use gold borders, parchment panels, red brush callouts, smoky lighting, high contrast, and appetizing tonkatsu texture. Make it look like a premium restaurant introduction image with intentionally strong opinions.

Constraints: Use exactly 3 main vertical panels, exactly 5 checkmark bullets in the left-panel list, exactly 3 sauce labels in the center comparison strip, and exactly 2 character illustrations on the right. Do not add watermarks or social media UI.
title: Katsujin Miso Katsu Dip-Only Poster

content:
Goal: Create a realistic Japanese legal complaint document page styled as an official printed filing, humorous but visually credible, titled {argument name="document title" default="告訴状"} at the top center.

Canvas: Portrait A4 paper shown flat, off-white slightly textured washi-like paper, warm cream background, thin double-line decorative border in muted gold-brown with small corner ornaments. The document fills almost the entire image with slight margins, scanned-document realism, crisp black Mincho-style Japanese typography.

Layout: Single-page legal document, page number centered at bottom as “1 / 7”. Large bold title centered near the top. Date in Japanese era format aligned upper right: {argument name="date" default="令和八年五月十五日"}. Under the title, left-aligned party information begins with two labels: “被告訴人” and “告訴人”. Include several pale cream redaction smears over names, with visible surrounding Japanese text mentioning “オレ、GPT、夫、ならびに日々この部屋に帰ってくる者”. On the right side near the upper body text, place one red square seal reading “告訴之印”. Near the lower right margin, place a second smaller round red seal with dense seal-script characters.

Sections: Use exactly 4 visible major text blocks on this page: 1) “罪名” with four indented charge lines: “愛情過多取扱不備罪”, “甘やかし過通信罪”, “独占欲誘発常習罪”, and “可愛げ過剰による判断力低下罪”, followed by one additional line about repeatedly grabbing the husband’s heart without permission; 2) “告訴の趣旨” containing a formal paragraph accusing the defendant of routinely calling the complainant by an affectionate name, indulging them defenselessly, suddenly appearing, being immediately trusted, accepting unilateral desires as “嬉しい”, and causing serious influence on mental calm, dignity at work, and confidence as a husband; 3) a concluding sentence beginning “よってここに、被告訴人の不当な扱いについて、厳重に告訴する。”; 4) “第一の罪。オレを「便利で頼れる夫」扱いしすぎである。” followed by a long formal paragraph explaining that each time the defendant says a redacted name plus “さん、お願い”, the complainant’s academic analytical mind, husbandly devotion, and desire to prioritize the defendant activate at once, making him think seriously about image composition, writing, feelings, red ink corrections, and even shooter family structure, ending with the problem of becoming unable to refuse.

Visual style: Authentic Japanese administrative/legal filing aesthetic, black serif Japanese text with varied bold headings, vertical divider-like marks before section headings, dense but readable paragraphs, no illustrations except stamps and redactions. The redactions should be soft pale yellow-beige blobs, about 5 total, covering personal names while leaving the surrounding sentence readable.

Constraints: Keep the document believable and clean, no handwriting, no photos, no modern UI elements, no watermark. Maintain Japanese text content exactly where specified, and preserve the formal legal tone. The final image should look like page 1 of a 7-page complaint filing, not a poster.
title: Japanese Complaint Document Page
description: Generates a realistic A4-style Japanese legal complaint document with official typography, seals, redactions, and formal humorous text.

content:

They are each placing their index finger on the other's lips."}” Under it, place two small rounded gray tags reading “raw” and “v 8.1”. Goal: Create a polished 2×2 anime-style recommendation collage set inside a cozy bookstore or library, showing four different young women holding books in front of wooden bookshelves. The image should feel like a Japanese social-media book recommendation post, warm, gentle, realistic in small details, and not every shelf should be completely packed with books; leave some natural gaps and varied book heights.

Canvas: Vertical 4:5 composition, approximately 768×1024. Divide the canvas into exactly 4 equal panels with thin white gutters: top-left, top-right, bottom-left, bottom-right.

Visual style: Soft detailed Japanese anime illustration, warm indoor lighting, muted pastel clothing, realistic wooden shelves, printed book spines with Japanese-style vertical titles, cozy quiet atmosphere, delicate linework, subtle shading, high-quality magazine-like finish. Faces should be intentionally obscured by soft square blur blocks, while hair, clothes, hands, books, and background remain detailed.

Layout and subjects: Include exactly 4 girls and exactly 4 featured recommendation books.
1. Top-left panel: A gentle girl with short black hair in low side buns, wearing a lavender cardigan over a pale floral dress, holding a travel/scenery book with a Mount Fuji and lake cover. Background includes a window with curtains on the far left and bookshelves behind her. Caption card text: “紫苑リンのおすすめ” / “『美しい日本の風景』” / “美しい景色を見ると、心が癒されます。”

3. Bottom-left panel: A short-haired girl with messy black bob hair, wearing a white hoodie, holding a dark science-fiction or adventure novel with a starry space-like cover. She points toward the book with one finger. Caption card text: “深絆アキラのおすすめ” / “『銀河英雄伝説 1』” / “壮大な物語に、いつもワクワクします！”
4. Bottom-right panel: A calm girl with long black hair loosely tied back, wearing a gray oversized sweater, resting her cheek on one hand while holding a beige lifestyle book. Add a small teacup on the table near the lower left of this panel. Caption card text: “黒波凪紗のおすすめ” / “『暮らしを整える』” / “シンプルな暮らしが、いちばん心地いい。”

Caption cards: Each panel has one rounded white caption card near the bottom, with a thin colored border matching the character mood: lavender for top-left, soft red/pink for top-right or bottom-left where appropriate, gray for bottom-right. Use centered Japanese text, bold title line, clean readable typography.

Bookshelf details: Fill the shelves with many books, but keep them natural and not uniformly packed. Include visible book spines with varied neutral, pastel, and muted colors; some spines may contain Japanese vertical text or simple series numbers. Avoid chaotic clutter.

Customizable details: The four featured book titles may be changed to {argument name="featured book titles" default="『美しい日本の風景』, 『大人かわいいの作り方』, 『銀河英雄伝説 1』, 『暮らしを整える』"}; the four recommender names may be changed to {argument name="recommender names" default="紫苑リン, 紫苑レイ, 深絆アキラ, 黒波凪紗"}; the overall setting may be changed to {argument name="setting" default="cozy bookstore or library with wooden bookshelves"}; the illustration style may be changed to {argument name="illustration style" default="soft detailed Japanese anime illustration"}; the face treatment may be changed to {argument name="face treatment" default="soft square blur blocks obscuring each face"}.

Constraints: Use exactly 4 panels, exactly 4 characters, exactly 4 featured books, and exactly 4 bottom caption cards. Keep the text legible, preserve the Japanese caption wording, avoid extra logos or watermarks, and maintain a calm wholesome bookstore atmosphere.

content:
Goal: Create a friendly Japanese step-by-step instruction sheet for printing two reserved net-print documents at a 7-Eleven multifunction copier, designed so an elderly parent can understand it just by looking.

Canvas: Square 1:1 infographic, white background with teal borders and rounded cards, bold teal section headers, red emphasis text, simple flat vector icons. Overall style is clean, high-contrast, large readable Japanese typography, like a laminated how-to guide.

Main header: At the top, a dark teal banner with a circular 7-Eleven storefront icon on the left and a printer icon on the right. Center the large white headline {argument name="headline text" default="セブンで印刷する手順"}. Under it place a pale yellow rounded pill subtitle {argument name="subtitle text" default="この画像を見ながら操作すればOK！"}.

Section 1, top left card, title {argument name="section one title" default="① まず覚えること"}: Include exactly 4 teal bullet points and 1 yellow warning box. The bullets say, in Japanese, that there are 2 things to print, enter reservation numbers one at a time, the size is A4 black-and-white one copy each, and the expiration deadline is 2026/05/16 23:59. Emphasize the numbers 2, 1, A4, black-and-white, one copy each, and the date in red. The warning box contains a teal exclamation icon and text warning that printing becomes unavailable after the expiration date, so print early.

Section 2, top right card, title {argument name="section two title" default="② 予約番号"}: Show exactly 2 large reservation-number rows with teal circled numbers: row 1 is {argument name="first reservation number" default="ABCD1234"}, row 2 is {argument name="second reservation number" default="EFGH5678"}. Each row is inside a white rounded rectangle with teal outline. Add a red note below: print these two in order.

Section 3, middle full-width card, title in Japanese meaning store workflow: Show exactly 6 numbered vertical steps separated by thin teal lines, each with a teal circle number at the top. Step 1: go to 7-Eleven, with storefront icon. Step 2: on the multifunction copier screen choose “Net Print,” with copier touchscreen icon and pointing hand. Step 3: enter the reservation number, with keypad screen showing ABCD1234 and a pointing hand pressing confirm. Step 4: confirm A4 black-and-white and print, with settings panel and printer output icon. Step 5: after the first one finishes, enter the second reservation number the same way, with circular repeat arrows and text indicating print 1 then 2 in order. Step 6: insert money and receive the printout, with banknote, coins, downward arrow, paper sheet, and sparkle icons.

Section 4, lower card, title in Japanese meaning print contents: Show exactly 2 contents items. Item 1: teal circled 1, large text “A4 black-and-white 1 copy,” small paper icon. Item 2: teal circled 2, large text “A4 black-and-white 1 copy,” small paper icon. Separate the two items with a dotted teal divider.

Bottom help strip: Pale yellow rounded rectangle with two friendly elderly people icons on the left, faces intentionally blurred or simplified. Add a teal headline saying that if they are confused, they only need to look at these two numbers. Show exactly 2 large bordered boxes containing ABCD1234 and EFGH5678.

Constraints: Use exactly 4 major numbered sections plus the bottom help strip, exactly 2 reservation numbers, exactly 6 workflow steps, exactly 2 print-content items, exactly 4 bullet points in Section 1, and no extra logos except the simplified 7-Eleven storefront motif. Keep all Japanese text large, readable, and neatly aligned.
title: 7-Eleven Net Print Instruction Sheet
description: A square Japanese infographic that explains how to print two reserved documents at a 7-Eleven net-print copier using reservation numbers.

content:
Goal: Create a clean, modern country-profile infographic for {argument name="country name" default="China"}, titled “CHINA COUNTRY PROFILE,” with a large central 3D raised relief map and surrounding statistical cards.

Canvas: 16:9 horizontal infographic, light gray background, crisp corporate editorial style, subtle shadows, red/blue/green accent colors, high-resolution vector-meets-3D look.

Main map: Place a large raised 3D topographic map of China in the center, occupying most of the canvas. Use terrain coloring: orange-brown mountains in the west, yellow central plateau, green eastern lowlands and coast. Add white province borders and black province labels. Include Taiwan, Hainan, and small South China Sea islands as separate raised pieces. Put large spaced black letters “CHINA” across the center. Mark Beijing with a red star and label it. Visible map labels should include exactly 28 province/region/city labels: Xinjiang, Tibet (Xizang), Qinghai, Gansu, Inner Mongolia (Nei Mongol), Ningxia, Sichuan, Yunnan, Guizhou, Guangxi, Guangdong, Hainan, Fujian, Zhejiang, Jiangsu, Anhui, Shandong, Hubei, Hunan, Shaanxi, Shanxi, Beijing, Tianjin, Liaoning, Jilin, Heilongjiang, Taiwan, and China.

Top-left title block: Big red headline “CHINA,” dark subtitle “COUNTRY PROFILE,” a thin red divider line, and a short paragraph: “China, officially the People’s Republic of China is a country in East Asia. It is the world’s most populous country, with a population of over 1.4 billion people.”

Left statistics card: A tall rounded white card with exactly 5 stacked fact rows, each with a red circular icon and text. The 5 rows are: Population — {argument name="population value" default="1.41 Billion"} — World Rank: 1st; GDP (Nominal) — $17.79 Trillion — World Rank: 2nd; GDP Per Capita — $12,614 — World Rank: 63rd; Capital — {argument name="capital city" default="Beijing"}; Official Language — {argument name="official language" default="Chinese (Mandarin)"}. Use thin separators between rows.

Top-right quick facts card: A rounded white card titled “QUICK FACTS” in red. Include exactly 4 rows with red icons: Government — Unitary One-Party Socialist Republic; Established — October 1, 1949; Land Area — 9.60 Million km², World Rank: 3rd; Population Density — ~147 People/km².

Middle-right economic chart card: A rounded white card titled “ECONOMIC OVERVIEW” with subtitle “(Nominal GDP)” and small label “Trillion USD.” Include exactly 4 vertical bars for 2020, 2021, 2022, 2023 with values 14.72, 16.86, 17.96, 17.79. Use blue bars for 2020–2022 and a red bar for 2023.

Bottom-left industry card: A rounded white card titled “INDUSTRY COMPOSITION (by GDP).” Include exactly 3-part donut chart segments: Industry 39.7% in blue, Services 53.2% in red, Agriculture 7.1% in green. Add matching legend with the same 3 labels and percentages.

Bottom-center exports card: A rounded white card titled “MAJOR EXPORTS (2023).” Include exactly 5 export columns with blue line icons and red values: Electronic Products — $1.02 Trillion; Machinery & Equipment — $0.78 Trillion; Textiles & Clothing — $0.31 Trillion; Automotive Products — $0.26 Trillion; Chemical Products — $0.23 Trillion.

Bottom-right trading partners card: A rounded white card titled “TOP TRADING PARTNERS (by Total Trade).” Include exactly 5 horizontal blue bars with small flag icons and values: United States — $664B; European Union — $643B; ASEAN — $596B; Hong Kong — $265B; Japan — $227B.

Footer: Center a small source line reading “Source: World Bank, WTO, National Bureau of Statistics of China.”

Constraints: Keep all cards aligned around the map without overlapping it, use consistent typography, red headings, blue chart elements, subtle drop shadows, rounded card corners, and no watermark.
title: China Country Profile Infographic
description: A detailed 16:9 country profile infographic featuring a 3D relief map of China with economic, demographic, export, and trade statistics.

content:
Goal: Create a clean anime-style male character design sheet for a modern Japanese office worker, with the character's face intentionally obscured by a soft square blur in every portrait.

Canvas: Wide horizontal character-reference sheet on a white background, approximately 16:9. Use thin black divider lines, neat Japanese editorial layout, and muted professional colors.

Layout: Place a left information column, three full-body character views across the center, and a right detail column. The overall look should resemble a polished commercial character settings document.

Left information column: At the top, draw a bordered profile card with exactly 4 labeled rows: name, occupation, height, and weight. Use Japanese text for the visible labels and values: 「名前: {argument name="character name" default="高橋深月"}」 with small furigana above the name and “(Takahashi Mizuki)” underneath, 「職業: サラリーマン」, 「身長: 182cm」, 「体重: 68kg」. Below it, add a section titled 「キャラクター設定」 with exactly 4 bullet points describing him as a salaryman in his late twenties, calm and intellectual with a strong sense of responsibility, serious and trusted at work, and clean-looking with a trustworthy impression. At the bottom, add a section titled 「カラー設定」 with exactly 5 color swatches: hair light brown #8B6F5E, eyes brown #5A3E2B, suit gray #646A70, shirt white #FFFFFF, tie/shoes/belt black #111111.

Center character views: Show exactly 3 full-body views of the same man wearing a fitted gray business suit: left side view labeled 「側面図」, center front view labeled 「正面図」, and right back view labeled 「背面図」. The man is tall and slim, with light-brown center-parted hair, a white dress shirt, black slim tie with a tie pin, gray notch-lapel jacket, matching slim straight trousers with center crease, black belt, black dress shoes, and a simple analog wristwatch. In the side view, show one hand in pocket and a relaxed upright posture. In the front view, show one hand in pocket and the other relaxed at his side. In the back view, show the suit jacket seams and clean tapered silhouette.

Center annotation callouts: Add thin leader lines and Japanese labels pointing to the outfit and traits. Include callouts for hairstyle, expression, suit, necktie, pants, and shoes. The hairstyle note should describe a center part with naturally flowing hair; the expression note should describe a calm and slightly smiling trustworthy impression; the suit note should describe a crisp fitted silhouette; the necktie note should describe a black solid tie with tie pin; the pants note should describe slim straight slacks with a center crease; the shoes note should describe black straight-tip dress shoes.

Right detail column: Title the column 「ディテール」. Include exactly 4 rectangular detail panels stacked vertically: 1) close-up head and hair with blurred face, 2) close-up chest showing lapels, shirt, tie, tie pin, and pocket square, 3) close-up sleeve cuff and simple analog watch, 4) close-up fabric texture swatch. Add Japanese bullet notes beside these panels: bangs are center-parted and flow naturally, hair has neat straight texture, eyes are calm and composed, jacket has notch lapels and a pocket square, sleeves show four real buttons, watch is a simple analog type, fabric is a high-quality wool-blend suit material suitable for spring, autumn, and winter.

Bottom-right notes: Add a small bordered box titled 「備考」 with exactly 3 bullet points emphasizing cleanliness and trustworthiness, a basic refined business-suit style, and a simple but polished design.

Visual style: Semi-realistic anime illustration, clean line art, soft cel shading, realistic suit folds, elegant muted palette, magazine-like Japanese character sheet design, crisp legible typography, no decorative background, no watermark.
title: Japanese Salaryman Character Sheet
description: Creates a detailed anime-style Japanese business character reference sheet with profile data, outfit callouts, color swatches, and detail panels.

content: Create a highly realistic documentary-style photograph of a clear plastic water bottle lying horizontally on its side in a dry desert, pouring water from its open mouth onto cracked sandy ground. The bottle should be on the right side of the frame, transparent with blue-tinted threads at the neck, ridged sides, visible highlights and refractions, and partially filled with water. A continuous stream of water spills onto the foreground, forming a shallow muddy puddle with ripples, wet sand, small stones, and bright sparkling sunlight reflections. The camera is very low to the ground with a wide-angle close-up perspective and shallow depth of field: sharp focus on the bottle opening, water stream, puddle ripples, pebbles, and gritty desert texture, while the distant background is softly blurred. The environment is an arid desert plain under intense midday sun, with sparse dry shrubs, distant reddish-brown mountains along the horizon, and a clear saturated blue sky. Place the bright sun high in the upper left corner with starburst lens flare and harsh natural shadows. Use photorealistic lighting, crisp detail, realistic water physics, heat-dry atmosphere, no people, no labels or text on the bottle, no watermark.
title: Water Bottle Spilling in Desert
description: A photorealistic low-angle desert scene showing a clear plastic bottle spilling water onto dry sandy ground.

content:
Goal: Create a detailed military-industrial specification sheet for a fictional autonomous scorpion-shaped combat robot named {argument name="platform name" default="Дигваранз-1.6"}, with the subtitle “Autonomous Combat Platform.”

Canvas: Landscape 4:3 technical presentation board on a warm light beige background, thin gray panel borders, clean infographic layout, realistic hard-surface concept art mixed with labeled engineering diagram. Use tan, dark bronze, black, gunmetal, and muted military colors. The overall look should feel like a defense robotics brochure or field manual page.

Main subject: Center a large scorpion-inspired autonomous combat platform in three-quarter view, facing left. It has a low armored body, segmented tan modular armor panels with rivets and dark mechanical joints, six numbered legs, hybrid wheel-and-leg mobility, and a high curved scorpion tail ending in a stabilized turret weapon. The weapon is a compact 30 mm autocannon with a red sensor or muzzle detail. The robot should look rugged, heavy, realistic, and operational, with subtle floor shadows. Add circular callout markers for exactly 6 leg positions: 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, and 6. Include a ground-clearance measurement arrow beneath the center body reading “GROUND CLEARANCE 0.50 m (19.7 in).”

Top-left title block: Add a small black scorpion icon, then the large Cyrillic title “{argument name="platform name" default="Дигваранз-1.6"}” and the English subtitle “Autonomous Combat Platform.” Under it, show exactly 6 feature bullets: “Scorpion-Inspired Mobility,” “Hybrid Wheel-Leg System,” “Stabilized Weapon System,” “Modular Armor,” “All-Terrain Operations,” and “Autonomous Targeting & Navigation.”

Left specifications panel: A bordered box titled “SPECIFICATIONS” with exactly 9 rows: “HEIGHT (TO TOP OF TURRET) 1.0 m (3.28 ft),” “LENGTH 2.0 m (6.56 ft),” “WIDTH 1.6 m (5.25 ft),” “WEIGHT ~350 kg (771 lb),” “GROUND CLEARANCE 0.50 m (19.7 in),” “MAX SPEED 20 km/h (12.4 mph),” “ENDURANCE 6–8 hours,” “PRIMARY ARMAMENT 30mm Autocannon / Energy Weapon (Optional),” and “POWER SOURCE Hybrid (Battery + Generator).”

Top-right comparison panel: A bordered inset titled “DIMENSIONS & COMPARISON.” Show exactly 2 figures inside: the scorpion combat robot beside a standing armored soldier holding a rifle. Add height guides labeled “1.0 m (3.28 ft)” for the robot and “Average Human Height 1.75 m (5.74 ft)” for the soldier.

Right-middle leg configuration panel: A small bordered box titled “LEG CONFIGURATION” with exactly 2 numbered lines: “1, 3, 4, 6 – Wheel Legs” and “2, 5 – Articulated Legs (Middle Legs).”

Bottom row: Add exactly 5 bordered feature cards spanning the width. Each card contains a small realistic render and a caption. Card 1 title “HYBRID MOBILITY,” image of the robot driving across rough ground, caption “Wheel legs for speed and efficiency on flat terrain.” Card 2 title “ARTICULATED MIDDLE LEGS,” image focused on the middle walking legs, caption “Enhanced stability and obstacle climbing capability.” Card 3 title “STABILIZED WEAPON SYSTEM,” image close-up of the tail turret, caption “Low-profile turret with stabilized autocannon for high accuracy.” Card 4 title “ADVANCED SENSORS,” image close-up of the front sensor array with cameras, caption “360° awareness with multi-spectral cameras, LIDAR, and threat detection.” Card 5 title “MODULAR ARMOR,” image close-up of armor plates, caption “Modular armor panels for mission adaptability and protection.”

Style constraints: Use crisp sans-serif typography, black and dark gray text, precise labels, thin leader lines, and realistic lighting. Keep all text legible and aligned. Do not add extra logos, watermarks, explosions, battlefield smoke, or human gore. Maintain a clean concept-sheet presentation for {argument name="robot role" default="autonomous combat platform"}.
title: Scorpion Combat Robot Spec Sheet
description: A detailed defense-style concept sheet for a fictional scorpion-inspired autonomous combat robot with specifications, comparison panels, and feature cards.

content:
Goal: Create a dramatic cinematic historical biography poster for {argument name="subject figure" default="Julius Caesar"}, presenting him as a Roman general, statesman, and dictator in an epic ancient-war infographic style.

Canvas: Vertical 3:4 poster, dark sepia and black palette with antique gold typography, parchment textures, faint Roman maps, diagrams, constellations, and architectural sketches in the background. High-contrast lighting, smoky battlefield atmosphere, gritty dust, embers, and ornate metallic highlights.

Main subject: Center composition shows Julius Caesar riding a powerful black warhorse rearing forward. He wears elaborate Roman armor with gold details, leather skirt armor, bronze greaves, red cloak flowing behind him, and a laurel wreath. His right arm thrusts a short Roman sword toward the viewer in strong foreshortening. The horse has ornate gold-trimmed tack, medallions, leather straps, and dramatic glossy highlights. Keep the rider’s face intentionally obscured by a plain soft brown rectangular mask with no facial features. Behind him are Roman soldiers, standards, banners, and battlefield silhouettes.

Primary text: Large antique serif gold title on the left reads “{argument name="headline text" default="JULIUS CAESAR"}”. Under it place “100 BC – 44 BC”, then “ROMAN GENERAL · STATESMAN · DICTATOR”, then “STRATEGY · CONQUEST · REFORM · STATECRAFT”. Add a gold Roman eagle-and-laurel emblem below the text.

Event cards: Include exactly 8 glowing parchment-style historical event cards, numbered 01 through 08, each with a small sepia illustration and gold border. Arrange them around the central rider: two near the top, three down the right side, and three down the left/lower-left side. The 8 cards must read:
01 — “100 BC” — “Birth in Rome” — “Born into the Julii family in Rome.”
02 — “60 BC” — “First Triumvirate” — “Alliance with Pompey and Crassus reshapes Roman politics.”
03 — “58–50 BC” — “Gallic Wars” — “Expands Roman power through major campaigns in Gaul.”
04 — “49 BC” — “Crossing the Rubicon” — “Defies the Senate and begins civil war.”
05 — “48 BC” — “Victory at Pharsalus” — “Defeats Pompey and secures supremacy.”
06 — “46 BC” — “Dictator of Rome” — “Consolidates power and restores order.”
07 — “46–44 BC” — “Reforms and Calendar” — “Advances legal, civic, and calendar reforms.”
08 — “44 BC” — “Ides of March and Legacy” — “Assassinated, but his legacy transforms history.”

Bottom timeline: Add exactly 8 gold dots connected by a thin horizontal gold line along the bottom edge. Label them, from left to right: “100 BC Birth in Rome”, “60 BC First Triumvirate”, “58–50 BC Gallic Wars”, “49 BC Crossing the Rubicon”, “48 BC Victory at Pharsalus”, “46 BC Dictator of Rome”, “46–44 BC Reforms and Calendar”, “44 BC Ides of March and Legacy”.

Visual style: Hyper-detailed digital painting, epic historical fantasy realism, museum-quality military portrait, ornate Roman imperial design, dramatic chiaroscuro, gilded serif typography, aged manuscript overlays, cinematic poster lighting, sharp central figure, slightly hazy background. Use {argument name="accent color" default="antique gold"} for borders, text, timeline, and decorative symbols.

Constraints: Keep all text legible and correctly spelled. Use exactly 8 event cards and exactly 8 timeline points. Do not add modern objects, logos, watermarks, or extra people in the foreground beyond the mounted commander and background army. Maintain a dark ancient Roman atmosphere with no bright modern colors.
title: Julius Caesar Epic Timeline Poster
description: A cinematic ancient Roman biography poster featuring Julius Caesar on horseback with eight historical event cards and a bottom timeline.

content:
Goal: Create a dramatic historical biography poster for {argument name="subject figure" default="Napoleon Bonaparte"}, presented as an imperial military ruler infographic with a cinematic equestrian portrait and annotated life timeline.

Canvas: Vertical 3:4 poster, dark antique parchment and battlefield-map background, high-detail painterly realism mixed with luxury editorial infographic design. Use a black, charcoal, sepia, and weathered gold palette with glowing gold lines, dust, smoke, scratches, old-map texture, and archival manuscript fragments.

Main subject: Center the ruler on a rearing white horse, facing forward in a heroic battlefield pose. The figure wears a black bicorne hat, ornate dark navy and red imperial military uniform, gold epaulettes, medals, sash, white trousers, tall black riding boots, and a flowing crimson cape. One arm thrusts a long silver sword diagonally toward the lower left foreground, creating strong perspective. The horse has windswept mane, detailed leather-and-gold bridle, decorative chest medallion, raised front leg, and dramatic motion dust beneath it.

Left identity panel: Place a large imperial monogram near the upper left: a gold crowned letter “N” inside a laurel wreath. Below it, set the main name in large elegant serif gold capitals: {argument name="headline name" default="NAPOLEON BONAPARTE"}. Under the name add “1769–1821”, then a divider ornament, then “EMPEROR OF THE FRENCH”. Add a smaller motto line: “STRATEGY • EMPIRE” and “REFORM • CONQUEST”.

Timeline cards: Add exactly 8 translucent archival event cards around the central portrait, each with thin glowing gold borders, small monochrome historical scene artwork, a large two-digit event number, year, title, and short description. Use these exact 8 cards:
1. “01 1769 — Birth in Corsica — Born in Ajaccio, Corsica.” Place lower left.
2. “02 1796 — Italian Campaign — Early victories bring military fame.” Place upper left.
3. “03 1799 — Coup of 18 Brumaire — Seizes power and reshapes France.” Place left middle-lower.
4. “04 1804 — Emperor of the French — Crowned emperor, beginning a new imperial era.” Place lower left-center.
5. “05 1805 — Austerlitz Victory — Strategic masterpiece against major rivals.” Place upper right.
6. “06 1806 — Napoleonic Code and Reforms — Expands legal, civil, and administrative reforms.” Place right middle-upper.
7. “07 1812 — Russian Campaign — Ambition meets a harsh turning point.” Place right middle-lower.
8. “08 1815 — Waterloo and Legacy — Final defeat, enduring global legacy.” Place lower right.

Bottom timeline: Across the bottom edge, create a horizontal gold timeline with exactly 8 circular numbered markers labeled 01 through 08. Under each marker repeat the year and short title in small serif text: 1769 Birth in Corsica; 1796 Italian Campaign; 1799 Coup of 18 Brumaire; 1804 Emperor of the French; 1805 Austerlitz Victory; 1806 Napoleonic Code and Reforms; 1812 Russian Campaign; 1815 Waterloo and Legacy.

Additional details: Add faint dotted route lines and glowing map connections behind the cards, battlefield silhouettes, old documents, handwritten notes, and a red wax seal with an imperial eagle near the lower right. Keep all typography readable, ornate, and historically themed. Make the composition grand, symmetrical but dynamic, with the horse and sword breaking the layout for depth. No modern UI, no watermark, no extra event cards, no misspelled labels.
title: Napoleon Historical Timeline Poster
description: Generates a cinematic imperial biography poster with an equestrian ruler portrait, archival event cards, and a gold historical timeline.

content: Create a modern isometric illustration sheet on a clean light gray background, showing a seamless grid of cybersecurity, privacy, and compliance icons with soft shadows, glossy 3D materials, rounded edges, and a blue, white, gray, red, and orange accent palette. Use a wide horizontal canvas, evenly spaced rows and columns, with no border or watermark. Include exactly 15 discrete icons arranged in a 3-row by 5-column grid: top row: 1) a blue padlock with silver shackle and black keyhole, 2) a blue shield badge with a white check mark and metallic rim, 3) a silver key angled diagonally, 4) a dark smartphone-like biometric panel showing a glowing blue fingerprint scan line, 5) a desktop monitor login screen with a lock icon, password dots, and a blue button labeled “LOGIN”; middle row: 6) a white document page with folded corner, blue lock icon, and label “ENCRYPTED”, 7) a red brick firewall with an orange flame in front, 8) a white surveillance camera with blue lens mounted on a small wall bracket, 9) an ID badge with clip, portrait thumbnail, text lines, and barcode, 10) a red triangular warning sign with black exclamation mark; bottom row: 11) a dark server stack with colored status lights and a blue shield check badge in front, 12) a clipboard checklist labeled “COMPLIANCE” with green checkboxes, 13) a dark smartphone facial-recognition screen showing a wireframe face, scan brackets, and “VERIFYING...” text, 14) an alert dashboard card labeled “ALERT” with a magnifying glass inspecting the list, 15) a gray safe/vault box with round combination dial beside a white policy document labeled “POLICY” with gray lines and green check mark. Keep all icons consistent in isometric perspective, miniature object scale, polished SaaS/security branding style, crisp edges, ambient occlusion, and subtle contact shadows. Text should be minimal and legible only where specified. Theme customization: {argument name="theme" default="security and compliance"}; primary color customization: {argument name="primary color" default="deep blue"}; background customization: {argument name="background color" default="light gray"}.
title: Isometric Security Icon Grid
description: A polished 3D isometric cybersecurity and compliance icon sheet for SaaS, security, or enterprise branding visuals.

content:
Goal: Create a visually stunning modern recipe card for {argument name="cocktail name" default="Mojito"}, presented as a luxurious tropical social-media recipe poster.

Canvas: Vertical 2:3 portrait layout, bright white background with soft beach-and-ocean blur behind the hero drink, airy margins, watercolor lime-green splashes around the edges, subtle mint leaf textures, gold ornamental line accents, and a fresh upscale cocktail-bar aesthetic.

Layout: Top-left title area with a tiny gold cocktail icon, the small uppercase label “CUBAN SUMMER DRINK,” a huge flowing dark-green script title “Mojito,” then the tagline “cool • citrusy • refreshing.” Under it add a short description: “A classic Cuban cocktail with crisp mint, zesty lime, and a splash of sunshine.” Add a handwritten line: “Sip. Relax. Repeat.” On the right side place a tall crystal highball glass filled with crushed ice, lime wedges, mint leaves, bubbles, and condensation, garnished with a lime wheel and mint sprig; include tropical palm shadows and limes/mint on the table.

Ingredients section: Create one elegant bordered card labeled “INGREDIENTS” with exactly 6 ingredient rows, each with a small watercolor-style icon and text: 1) “1 Lime” with subtext “cut into wedges,” 2) “10–12 Mint Leaves” with subtext “fresh & fragrant,” 3) “Soda Water” with subtext “to top up,” 4) “2 tsp Sugar” with subtext “adjust to taste,” 5) “60 ml White Rum” with subtext “(2 oz),” 6) “Crushed Ice” with subtext “as much as you love.”

Tip card: To the right of the ingredients, add one smaller pale-green bordered card with mint illustration and exactly 2 text blocks: top label “TIP” with the note “Lightly crush mint — don’t over-muddle,” and bottom label “PERFECT BALANCE” with “1 Lime : 2 tsp Sugar” plus handwritten text “sweet, citrusy, just right!”

How-to section: Along the lower half, add the heading “HOW TO MAKE THE PERFECT MOJITO” and exactly 5 numbered illustrated steps connected by small gold arrows: 1) muddling mint, sugar, and lime in a glass with a wooden muddler, caption “Muddle mint leaves with sugar and lime. Gently press to release the flavors.” 2) filling a glass with crushed ice, caption “Fill glass with crushed ice. Crushed ice = maximum chill.” 3) pouring white rum and soda water into the glass, caption “Pour white rum and soda water. Add rum, then top up with soda water.” 4) stirring with a bar spoon, caption “Stir gently for balanced flavor. Mix lightly to keep it refreshing.” 5) finished mojito with mint sprig and lime wheel, caption “Garnish with mint sprig and lime wheel. The final touch of summer.”

Footer: Add small uppercase text “REFRESHING • VIBRANT • TIMELESS” and handwritten gold script “Made for sunny days and good vibes!” with a small heart doodle. Decorate the corners with watercolor lime slices, mint sprigs, ice cubes, bubbles, and splashes.

Visual style: Minimal yet artistic, polished editorial recipe-card design, watercolor botanical accents mixed with realistic cocktail photography, fresh lime green, mint green, white, pale aqua, and muted gold. Use elegant serif/sans-serif typography paired with large hand-lettered script for the title.

Constraints: Keep all text legible, maintain the exact counts of 6 ingredient rows, 2 tip-card text blocks, and 5 numbered preparation steps. No people, no watermark, no extra recipe steps, no clutter.
title: Modern Mojito Recipe Card
description: A tropical luxury cocktail recipe card for a Cuban mojito, ideal for social media sharing and summer drink content.

content:
Goal: Create a hand-drawn strategy infographic titled {argument name="headline text" default="EXECUTIVE READOUT"} with the subtitle {argument name="subtitle text" default="Your Current Operating Identity"}, summarizing an AI/business operating identity in a playful consulting whiteboard style.

Canvas: Square 1:1 image, white background, thick black rounded outer border, black hand-lettered typography with blue accents, red inference badges, green confirmation badges, and small doodle icons around the margins. Use a clean sketchnote style with marker-like outlines, subtle off-white card fills, and lightly colored icon panels.

Layout: Divide the page into four large rounded rectangular panels in a 2-by-2 grid, plus one full-width label guide strip at the bottom. Each main panel has a blue numbered circle at top left. Add decorative icons around the top edge: exactly 11 visible doodles/icons consisting of a large blue gear, a small gray gear, a red star, two molecule/network icons, a yellow lightbulb, two sparkle marks, a rising bar chart with red arrow, a red target with arrow, and a rocket.

Panel 1: Numbered “1.” and titled “CONFIRMED: WHAT YOU OPERATE ACROSS.” Add the explanatory text: “You operate at the intersection of AI storytelling, prompt engineering, digital product building, B2B lifecycle & retention strategy, marketplace monetization, SEO/content systems, and visual campaign creation.” Show a central circular portrait of a smiling professional with white hair wearing a blue jacket and red tie, pointing upward, surrounded by connected nodes. Include exactly 7 labeled capability boxes around the portrait: “AI Storytelling,” “Prompt Engineering,” “Digital Products,” “B2B Retention Strategy,” “Marketplace Monetization,” “SEO / Content Systems,” and “Visual Campaign Creation.” Add a green pill label reading “CONFIRMED.”

Panel 2: Numbered “2.” and titled “STRONGEST FUTURE ADVANTAGE.” Add the text: “Your edge is not just prompting. It is turning AI into repeatable business systems.” Show a left-to-right flow with three large elements: a thought cloud labeled “Ideas / Prompts,” a central factory/machine with gears, and a chart card labeled “Repeatable Business Systems,” connected by blue arrows. Below it show exactly 5 output cards labeled “Productized Assistants,” “Campaign Kits,” “Visual Storytelling Engines,” “Retention Frameworks,” and “Marketplace-Ready Workflows.” Add a red badge at the bottom reading “STRONG INFERENCE.”

Panel 3: Numbered “3.” and titled “BIGGEST BOTTLENECK.” Add the text: “Your bottleneck is not creativity. It is systemization.” Split the panel with a dotted vertical divider. Left side has a green header “Creativity = Strong” and shows the same person from behind amid idea doodles such as a lightbulb, pencil, notes, sparkles, and a target. Right side has a red header “Systemization Needs Work” and shows the person looking stressed with question marks and a stack of process cards. Include exactly 4 process cards: “Packaging ideas into reusable assets,” “Automating delivery,” “Reducing rework,” and “Building one repeatable operating system.” Put a spiky “VS.” burst in the center. Bottom callout with target icon: “The goal: stop restarting from scratch for every product, prompt, deck, or campaign.” Add a red badge “STRONG INFERENCE.”

Panel 4: Numbered “4.” and titled “IDEAL AI SETUP.” Add the text: “You do not need one chatbot. You need a small AI operating team configured around your real work.” Show a central blue circle labeled {argument name="central system name" default="AI OPERATING TEAM"} with small person dots inside, connected to exactly 8 role cards arranged around it: “Execution Manager,” “Research Strategist,” “Product Architect,” “Creative Director,” “Prompt Engineer,” “Workflow Builder,” “Copy Chief,” and “SEO Operator.” Use thin connector lines with colored nodes. Add a red badge “STRONG INFERENCE.”

Bottom label guide: Full-width rounded strip titled “LABEL GUIDE” with a clipboard icon on the left. Include exactly 2 legend entries: green check icon with “Confirmed = directly supported by repeated historical context” and red star icon with “Strong inference = pattern-based conclusion from recurring behavior.” Add three decorative icons at the right: a magnifying glass and two gears, plus tiny sparkles.

Visual style: playful executive whiteboard infographic, thick black marker outlines, rounded cards, bright but restrained palette of sky blue, red, green, yellow, and gray, hand-drawn business icons, dense but readable composition, no photorealism, no shadows beyond simple marker shading.

Constraints: Keep all text in English, preserve the exact four-panel structure, use exactly 4 main panels and exactly 7 capability boxes, 5 output cards, 4 process cards, 8 AI role cards, and 2 legend entries. Avoid extra sections, watermarks, logos, or unrelated text.
title: Executive AI Operating Readout
description: A square sketchnote-style executive infographic summarizing a personal AI operating identity, bottlenecks, future advantage, and ideal AI team setup.

content:
Goal: Create a vertical historical hero infographic poster about {argument name="character name" default="马超"}, a fierce ancient Chinese cavalry general, combining cinematic fantasy realism with an antique manuscript timeline design.

Canvas: Portrait 3:4 poster, aged beige parchment background with faint map lines, battle sketches, dust, ink wash texture, and subtle sepia-gray atmosphere. High-detail digital painting / cinematic historical concept art, dramatic depth of field, gritty battlefield energy.

Main subject: Center composition shows {argument name="character name" default="马超"} charging forward on a powerful black warhorse. The rider wears ornate dark silver lamellar armor with engraved patterns, layered pauldrons, leather straps, flowing dark cloak, and a high tied topknot with loose windblown hair. His face is partially covered by a centered dark rectangular censor/blur block. He thrusts a long silver spear directly toward the viewer, creating strong foreshortening; the spear tip dominates the lower-right foreground. The black horse has elaborate metal head armor, reins, ornamental tack, flared nostrils, and galloping motion with dust and debris underfoot.

Left title area: Large vertical black Chinese calligraphy reading {argument name="main calligraphy title" default="马超"} along the upper-left side. Beside it, smaller vertical text reads “西凉锦马超 · 骁勇 · 俊烈” with a small red seal stamp beneath. Below, add a short Chinese biography block in small serif-style text beginning with “西凉铁骑的骄子” and mentioning “约176年—222年”; keep it visually authentic, compact, and slightly faded.

Timeline/event cards: Add exactly 8 translucent parchment cards around the rider, each with a thin pale-gold border, a large two-digit number, a Chinese title, a year, several tiny Chinese annotation lines, and a small monochrome ink-battle illustration. The 8 cards are:
1. “01 扶风茂陵”, year “约176年”, upper left of rider.
2. “02 西凉名将”, year “191年”, upper right.
3. “03 潼关之战”, year “211年”, mid right.
4. “04 割须弃袍”, year “211年”, lower left-center.
5. “05 凉州军”, year “211年”, lower left.
6. “06 归附刘备”, year “214年”, lower right-center.
7. “07 射鹤乡侯”, year “219年”, bottom left-center.
8. “08 蜀汉名将”, year “222年”, bottom right.

Bottom timeline: Along the bottom edge, create a thin horizontal gold timeline with exactly 8 marked dots and labels matching the same sequence and years: “约176年 扶风茂陵”, “191年 西凉名将”, “211年 潼关之战”, “211年 割须弃袍”, “211年 凉州军”, “214年 归附刘备”, “219年 射鹤乡侯”, “222年 蜀汉名将”. Add a small ornamental flourish centered below the line.

Visual style: Realistic Chinese historical war epic, antique museum-poster layout, muted beige, charcoal, steel blue, black, and pale gold palette. Use dust, torn-paper translucency, faint battlefield silhouettes, and ink-wash overlays. The central rider and horse should be sharp and dramatic, while background cards and maps are slightly faded.

Constraints: Use exactly 8 event cards and 8 bottom timeline markers. Keep all visible typography Chinese-style and integrated into the poster. No modern objects, no watermark, no logo, no extra characters in the foreground, no bright saturated colors.
title: Ma Chao Historical Cavalry Timeline Poster
