content:
Goal: Create a dramatic vertical historical-warrior poster infographic about {argument name="character name" default="吕布"}, the legendary Three Kingdoms general, combining cinematic fantasy realism with aged Chinese manuscript design.

Canvas: Vertical 3:4 poster, parchment-gray textured background with ink wash stains, dust, smoke, battlefield grit, and faint cracks. Use a high-contrast cinematic composition with the main figure exploding forward from the center.

Main subject: A powerful armored Chinese cavalry general charges directly toward the viewer on a dark chestnut warhorse. The rider wears ornate dark bronze and black lamellar armor with red cloth accents, chains, metal shoulder guards, and an elaborate crown/helmet with two extremely long red pheasant-plume antennae rising high and curving outward. His face is intentionally obscured by a soft rectangular blur/mask while the rest of the image is sharp. He thrusts a massive ornate halberd toward the camera, creating extreme foreground perspective: the weapon blade is huge, blurred by depth of field, and extends from the lower left toward the rider’s hand. The horse has windblown reddish mane, decorated bridle, metal ornaments, tassels, and raised front legs, kicking up mud and smoke.

Left typography: Large black calligraphic Chinese title {argument name="main title" default="吕布"} arranged vertically on the left side, bold brush texture. Beside it place a smaller vertical phrase {argument name="vertical subtitle" default="飞将 · 猛将 · 乱世枭雄"}. Add red seal stamps and a short biography block in Chinese beneath the title, including “三国第一猛将”, “约160年–199年”, and several lines of small serif Chinese text.

Infographic panels: Include exactly 7 semi-transparent parchment event cards around the central rider, each with a thin pale-gold rectangular border, a large two-digit number, Chinese event title, year, small descriptive Chinese lines, and a monochrome sketch-like battle illustration. The 7 visible cards are: 01 “九原出身” with “约160年” near the upper left; 02 “并州骁将” with “184年” near the upper right; 03 “诛董卓” with “189年” at mid-left; 04 “虎牢关之战” with “190年” at mid-right; 05 “辕门射戟” with “191年” at lower left; 06 “下邳之战” with “194年” at lower right; 07 “白门楼殒命” with “199年” at bottom right. Keep the panels slightly tilted in perspective as if floating behind the warrior, partially obscured by smoke and the horse.

Bottom timeline: Add exactly 7 timeline milestones across the bottom edge in muted gold, matching the cards: “约160年 九原出身”, “184年 并州从军”, “189年 诛董卓”, “190年 虎牢关之战”, “191年 辕门射戟”, “194年 下邳之战”, “199年 白门楼殒命”. Use small dots connected by a thin horizontal line, with a subtle decorative flourish centered below.

Visual style: Hyper-detailed cinematic Chinese historical fantasy, realistic armor and horse anatomy, dynamic motion, shallow depth of field, smoky battlefield atmosphere, muted sepia parchment palette with black ink, dark bronze, oxblood red, and pale gold highlights. Make the background panels look like archival sketches printed on old paper.

Constraints: Use Chinese text as visible graphic elements, keep the exact count of 7 event cards and 7 timeline milestones, no modern objects, no logos or watermarks, no extra characters in the foreground, and maintain a poster-like editorial infographic layout.
title: Lü Bu Historical Warrior Poster
description: A cinematic Chinese historical infographic poster featuring Lü Bu charging on horseback with seven annotated life-event panels and a bottom timeline.

content:
Goal: Create a dramatic Chinese historical-warrior infographic poster about {argument name="character name" default="Zhao Yun"}, showing a heroic armored cavalry general charging toward the viewer on a white horse, surrounded by semi-transparent holographic chronology panels.

Canvas: Vertical poster, 3:4 aspect ratio, cinematic high detail, cool gray-blue palette, smoky battlefield background, dust and debris at the bottom, bright rim lighting and icy glowing UI edges. The composition is dense but readable, with the mounted warrior centered and the horse and spear projecting forward in strong perspective.

Main subject: A Chinese Three Kingdoms-style general in ornate silver lamellar armor and layered white-gray robes rides a powerful white horse. He has long flowing silver-white hair tied with a decorative crown. The face area is deliberately obscured by a soft rectangular blur. He grips an extremely long silver spear angled from upper left toward the lower right foreground, with the spearhead close to the viewer. The horse wears elaborate silver tack, engraved bridle plates, tassels, and a chest ornament; its mane blows wildly. Mood: fearless, legendary, stormy, heroic.

Left title column: Large black ink-brush calligraphy reading {argument name="main calligraphy" default="赵云"}. Beside it, vertical text reads “常胜将军 · 独胆英雄 · 银枪白马”. Add a small red seal stamp reading “子龙”. Below, include a short biography block titled “蜀汉名将” with small Chinese body text describing him as a lifelong loyal general of the Shu Han, called the “常胜将军”, with an eight-foot stature, majestic appearance, and a silver spear in hand.

Floating chronology panels: Use exactly 8 translucent glass-like rectangular panels with cyan glowing borders, each containing a numbered title, several short Chinese lines, a year, and a small monochrome battle illustration in the background. Arrange them around the rider without covering the main horse head or spear: upper left, upper right, mid left, mid right, lower left, lower right, bottom left, bottom right. The 8 visible panels are: 01 “初出茅庐”, includes “随公孙瓒 / 建功立业 / 约184年”; 02 “长坂救救主”, includes “曹操大军来袭 / 七进七出 / 救出刘备幼子阿斗 / 208年”; 03 “汉水之战”, includes “以少胜多 / 大破曹军 / 晋夺城 / 208年”; 04 “单骑救阿斗”, includes “单枪匹马 / 深入敌阵 / 护阿斗安全脱险 / 208年”; 05 “智取桂阳”, includes “智取赵范 / 平定桂阳 / 功勋卓著 / 214年”; 06 “助攻取益州”, includes “随军入川 / 攻城拔寨 / 立下赫赫战功 / 214年”; 07 “箕谷之战”, includes “镇守箕谷 / 击退魏军 / 保蜀汉后方 / 228年”; 08 “北伐中原”, includes “随诸葛亮北伐 / 屡建奇功 / 威震中原 / 228年–231年”.

Bottom timeline: Add a thin gold horizontal timeline with exactly 7 marked date points and labels: “?–184 常山真定人”, “184 随公孙瓒 初出茅庐”, “208 长坂坡之战 救主立功”, “214 智取桂阳 助攻益州”, “228 箕谷之战 北伐开始”, “231 北伐中原 屡建奇功”, “229–? 病逝成都 追谥顺平侯”.

Visual style: Ultra-detailed fantasy-realistic Chinese historical illustration mixed with futuristic transparent infographic overlays, metallic armor detail, painterly smoke, engraved textures, cold glowing highlights, high contrast, sharp spear perspective, epic poster design. Avoid modern weapons, avoid cartoon style, avoid extra panels, avoid English text in the artwork.
title: Zhao Yun Cavalry Infographic Poster
description: Creates an epic Chinese historical fantasy poster of Zhao Yun charging on a white horse with eight glowing chronology panels and a bottom timeline.

content:
Goal: Create a vertical historical Chinese epic infographic poster about {argument name="character name" default="Guan Yu"}, combining a cinematic Three Kingdoms warrior portrait with parchment-style chronology panels.

Canvas: Tall portrait poster, 3:4 aspect ratio, aged beige rice-paper background with ink wash stains, faint mountains, smoke, battlefield silhouettes, and a museum-scroll texture. Overall palette is sepia, charcoal, dark olive green, bronze, and muted blood-brown.

Main subject: A hyper-detailed armored Chinese general, {argument name="character name" default="Guan Yu"}, dominates the center, riding a powerful dark brown war horse. He wears layered dark green robes, ornate blackened bronze lamellar armor, engraved shoulder guards, leather straps, metal studs, and a green headwrap with long ribbons streaming to the right. Long black hair and beard flow in the wind; the face area is intentionally softened/obscured like a smooth blurred mask, while the armor and clothing remain sharply detailed. He holds a massive ornate guandao polearm angled from the center toward the lower left foreground; the crescent blade is oversized, silver, engraved with gold filigree, and strongly foreshortened. The horse has glossy musculature, an alert expression, ornate bridle, bronze medallions, decorative leather tack, and one raised front leg, facing slightly right.

Left title area: Place a huge vertical black ink-brush calligraphy title reading {argument name="large calligraphy title" default="关羽"} on the upper left. Beside it, add smaller vertical text in Chinese reading “武圣·忠义·威震华夏”, with a small red seal stamp below. Under the title, include a short biographical text block: “关羽生平 / 约160年—220年 / 字云长，本字长生 / 河东解县（今山西运城）人 / 蜀汉名将，五虎上将之首 / 以忠义仁勇著称 / 后世尊为 武圣 / 与‘文圣’孔子齐名”.

Chronology panels: Use exactly 8 translucent rectangular parchment cards arranged around the main rider, each with a large two-digit number, a bold Chinese event title, a year, 2–4 lines of smaller Chinese notes, and a faded monochrome battle illustration behind the text. The cards should look like layered glass-paper frames with thin white borders and ink-wash war scenes.
1. Top center-left card: “01 桃园结义”, year “约184年”, showing three sworn brothers under trees.
2. Mid left card: “02 温酒斩华雄”, year “190年”, showing generals and soldiers near a battlefield standard.
3. Lower left card: “03 过五关斩六将”, year “200年”, showing riders passing through a gate or fortress battle.
4. Bottom left card: “04 单刀赴会”, year “215年”, showing a dramatic riverside or military meeting scene.
5. Top right card: “05 水淹七军”, year “219年”, showing flooded armies, ships, and chaos.
6. Upper-mid right card: “06 刮骨疗毒”, year “219年”, showing a medical treatment scene with soldiers nearby.
7. Mid-lower right card: “07 威震华夏”, year “219年”, showing a smoky battlefield and massed troops.
8. Bottom right card: “08 败走麦城”, year “220年”, showing a seated general and tragic final-campaign atmosphere.

Bottom timeline: Add a thin horizontal timeline along the entire bottom edge with exactly 8 labeled points matching the events: “约160年 生于河东解县”, “184年 桃园结义”, “190年 温酒斩华雄”, “200年 过五关斩六将”, “215年 单刀赴会”, “219年 水淹七军”, “219年 威震华夏”, “220年 败走麦城”. Use small gold dots connected by a fine line, with compact serif Chinese captions.

Visual style: Epic historical fantasy realism, Chinese ink-wash poster design, cinematic lighting, high-detail metal engraving, smoky atmosphere, battlefield dust, layered parchment collage, elegant typography. The central rider and horse are high-contrast and sharp; the background cards are slightly faded and desaturated.

Constraints: Keep exactly 8 chronology cards and exactly 8 bottom timeline points. Do not add modern objects, logos, watermarks, or extra characters in the foreground. Preserve the Chinese text content as visible poster typography, while all composition instructions remain in English.
title: Guan Yu Historical Infographic Poster
description: A cinematic Three Kingdoms poster showing Guan Yu on horseback with eight illustrated chronology panels and a bottom timeline.

content:
Goal: Create a realistic screenshot of an AI chat interface showing a generated technical infographic about {argument name="topic" default="how Large Language Models (LLMs) work technically"}. The screenshot should look like a modern web app conversation, not a standalone poster.

Canvas: 768×1024 vertical screenshot, light gray app background, rounded white content areas, clean sans-serif typography, subtle shadows, high-resolution but with the infographic text slightly small like a real embedded generated image.

Chat UI layout: At the top left show a small circular user avatar, the chat title “Visualizing LLM Architecture” with a tiny dropdown chevron, and at the top right a simple “Files” label with an icon. Below, show a rounded user message bubble aligned near the top center/right containing: “make an image explaining how LLMs work technically”. Under it, show a small status row reading “Scira task complete” with a sparkle/loader icon and chevron. The main generated image appears below as a large rounded rectangle card. Beneath the image, include assistant explanatory text: “The image above is a comprehensive technical infographic breaking down how Large Language Models function under the hood. Here is a detailed walkthrough of each component shown:” followed by the bold section heading “Tokenization: From Text to Numbers”. At the bottom, show a rounded input box with placeholder “Ask a follow-up...”, a plus button on the left, small tool/model controls on the right, the model label “Kimi K2.6” with a dropdown, and a circular voice button.

Generated infographic inside the chat: Design a blue-and-white technical educational poster titled in large navy caps: “HOW LARGE LANGUAGE MODELS (LLMs) WORK”. Use a white background, navy-blue outlines, light-blue highlights, rounded panels, arrows connecting steps, miniature charts, equations, tables, and icons. The poster should be information-dense and engineering-oriented.

Infographic sections: Use exactly 8 labeled panels/areas:
1. “INPUT: TOKENIZATION” panel showing a raw text box with the sentence “The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog.”, a tokenizer block, token boxes for the words, and token ID boxes.
2. “EMBEDDINGS” panel showing token IDs converted into dense vectors, with a small table of numeric embedding values.
3. “TRANSFORMER ARCHITECTURE” panel showing a stacked transformer block with Add & Norm, Feed-Forward Network, Multi-Head Self-Attention, input embeddings, positional encoding, and layer repetition notation.
4A. “SELF-ATTENTION MECHANISM (INSIDE ONE HEAD)” wide lower-left panel showing matrices for input embeddings, queries, keys, values, attention scores, softmax, attention weights, weighted sum, and equations.
4B. “ATTENTION: TOKENS ATTEND TO EACH OTHER” panel showing a network graph of tokens from the example sentence connected by blue lines plus attention-weight bars.
5. “OUTPUT: NEXT TOKEN PREDICTION” panel showing probability distribution bars for candidate next tokens such as cat, sat, on, the, mat, roof, then highlighting the predicted next token “the”.
6. “TRAINING: PRE-TRAINING WITH NEXT-TOKEN PREDICTION” long bottom strip divided into 5 mini-cards: massive text corpus, creating training examples, model prediction, loss calculation, and backpropagation/update.
7. Bottom process arrow reading “Repeat for billions of examples over many epochs until convergence.”
8. Bottom-right result callout with a brain icon explaining that the model learns general language patterns and knowledge.

Visual style: Crisp vector infographic, academic but friendly, dark navy headings, medium-blue borders, pale-blue fills, tiny tables and plots, clean arrows, rounded cards, consistent spacing. Make the embedded infographic resemble an AI-generated educational diagram with dense but mostly legible small text.

Constraints: Keep all UI text in English. Do not add watermarks. Preserve the visible chat screenshot framing and the large embedded infographic. Use exactly the listed 8 infographic areas and exactly 5 mini-cards inside the training strip.
title: LLM Architecture Chat Screenshot
description: Creates a realistic AI chat screenshot featuring a dense blue-and-white technical infographic explaining how large language models work.

content:
Goal: Create a clean reference-sheet infographic titled {argument name="headline text" default="FACS Action Unit Reference Grid"}, showing a realistic female character expression sheet for Facial Action Coding System action units.

Canvas: Square 1:1 white canvas, approximately 1024 x 1024 px, with a centered black title at the top and a smaller subtitle reading {argument name="subtitle text" default="Realistic Female Character Expression Sheet"}. Use a plain academic reference-chart layout.

Layout: Below the title, create an exact 7-column by 7-row grid with thin light-gray borders, for exactly 49 individual portrait cells. Each cell contains one small bust portrait centered above a tiny caption line. Keep spacing uniform and margins narrow. The portraits should all be the same realistic young adult woman with {argument name="hair style" default="shoulder-length black hair with a center part, slightly wavy ends"}, wearing a simple dark top, front-facing unless the label requires a head turn, tilt, up, down, forward, or back. The face area should be softly anonymized or blurred with a skin-tone rectangular blur while preserving hair silhouette, head angle, and overall expression-reference pose.

Visual style: Photorealistic character-sheet thumbnails, neutral studio lighting, white background, consistent scale, realistic hair detail, minimal shadows, scientific reference grid aesthetic. Typography is small black sans-serif text; captions should fit inside each cell.

Text content: Include exactly 49 labeled cells, numbered left to right, top to bottom. Use these exact visible English labels in order: 1. AU1 Inner Brow Raiser; 2. AU2 Outer Brow Raiser; 3. AU4 Brow Lowerer; 4. AU5 Upper Lid Raiser; 5. AU6 Cheek Raiser; 6. AU7 Lid Tightener; 7. AU9 Nose Wrinkler; 8. AU10 Upper Lip Raiser; 9. AU12 Lip Corner Puller; 10. AU14 Dimpler; 11. AU15 Lip Corner Depressor; 12. AU17 Chin Raiser; 13. AU18 Lip Pucker; 14. AU20 Lip Stretcher; 15. AU23 Lip Tightener; 16. AU25 Lips Part; 17. AU26 Jaw Drop; 18. AU27 Mouth Stretch; 19. AU41 Lid Droop; 20. AU42 Slit Eyes; 21. AU43 Eyes Closed; 22. AU44 Squint; 23. AU45 Blink; 24. AU46 Wink; 25. AU51 Head Turn Left; 26. AU52 Head Turn Right; 27. AU53 Head Up; 28. AU54 Head Down; 29. AU55 Head Tilt Left; 30. AU56 Head Tilt Right; 31. AU57 Head Forward; 32. AU58 Head Back; 33. AU61 Eyes Left; 34. AU62 Eyes Right; 35. AU63 Eyes Up; 36. AU64 Eyes Down; 37. AU82 Nostril Dilator; 38. AU83 Nostril Compressor; 39. AU85 Tongue Out; 40. AU1+2 Surprise Brows; 41. AU4+7 Tense Eyes; 42. AU6+12 Genuine Smile; 43. AU12+25 Open Smile; 44. AU15+17 Sad Mouth; 45. AU14+4 Worried Brow; 46. AU9+10 Disgust; 47. AU20+25 Fear Tension; 48. Neutral Face; 49. Comparison Face.

Subject details: The character is {argument name="character description" default="a realistic young East Asian woman"}. Maintain consistent identity, hair, lighting, and crop across all 49 cells, with only the action unit, gaze direction, or head pose changing according to the caption.

Constraints: Use exactly 49 cells and no extra thumbnails. Keep the chart monochrome and white aside from realistic portrait colors. Do not add logos, watermarks, decorative icons, or extra explanatory paragraphs. Make the title large and readable, while individual captions are tiny but structured like a reference grid.
title: FACS Action Unit Reference Grid
description: Generates a square 49-cell realistic female FACS expression reference sheet for studying or prompting facial action units.

content:
Goal: Create a clean sci-fi technical cutaway infographic for a No Man's Sky–inspired facility titled {argument name="headline text" default="PLANETARY SURVEY OUTPOST"}, showing a hardened planetary survey base with a large central dome and swept wing structures, as if from an aerospace architecture manual.

Canvas: Wide landscape 16:9 poster on a white background, high-resolution concept-art rendering combined with crisp editorial diagram typography. Use muted olive-gray armor panels, dark graphite structural ribs, warm interior lighting, and purple sensor glow accents.

Main layout: Place the huge facility cross-section across the center, spanning nearly the full width. The center is a semi-circular reinforced dome sliced open to reveal 4 stacked interior levels: 1) top sensor and telescope chamber, 2) crew/lab rooms, 3) operations/control floor with blue monitors, 4) lower power/storage machinery bay. Attach two broad angular wings: the left wing is pale green reinforced armor, the right wing is darker with an open cutaway sensor bay near the tip. Add thin leader lines and small technical labels around the structure.

Top-left text block: Under the headline, add the subtitle {argument name="subtitle text" default="CUTAWAY DIAGRAM"}. Include a short paragraph: "A hardened, low-profile facility built for long-term planetary observation and data collection. The reinforced dome and wing structures house sensors, living quarters, and support systems while withstanding harsh surface conditions."

Callouts: Include exactly 9 labeled callouts around the main cutaway, each with a short description: 1) "REINFORCED WING STRUCTURE" — composite armor and load-bearing struts provide durability and impact resistance; 2) "OBSERVATION BAYS" — deployable sensor pods for atmospheric and terrain analysis; 3) "CENTRAL SENSOR DOME" — rotating sensor array and high-resolution scanning instruments; 4) "COMMUNICATION ARRAY" — high-gain antenna systems for long-range data transmission; 5) "HABITATION DECK" — crew quarters, galley, recreation, and medical facilities; 6) "OPERATIONS DECK" — command center, data analysis labs, and mission planning; 7) "POWER CORE" — compact fission reactor providing power to all systems; 8) "STORAGE & WORKSHOP" — equipment storage, fabrication lab, and maintenance bay; 9) "DEPLOYABLE SENSOR POD" — autonomous pod for atmospheric sampling, terrain mapping, and surface scanning.

Inset panels: Add exactly 3 framed inset panels. Bottom-left panel shows a separate small render of a deployable sensor pod on landing skids labeled "DEPLOYABLE SENSOR POD". Upper-right panel is a "SPECIFICATIONS" card with exactly 6 rows: Diameter (Dome) ~ 68 m; Wingspan ~ 140 m; Height ~ 35 m; Mass ~ 24,000 tonnes; Crew Capacity 24; Power Output ~ 3.2 MW. Bottom-right panel is a top-view facility layout labeled "FACILITY LAYOUT (TOP VIEW)", showing the round central dome and two wings, with labels for Left Wing (Sensor Bays), Right Wing (Comm Array & Support), and Central Dome (Operations & Core).

Visual style: Realistic sci-fi concept art mixed with technical blueprint infographic design, sharp cross-section details, small human-scale rooms and equipment, subtle shadows under the base, thin purple-gray leader lines, compact uppercase sans-serif labels, and a polished magazine-spread composition.

Constraints: Keep all text in English, make the diagram readable and information-dense, do not add logos, watermarks, people in the foreground, or extra inset panels. The subject should feel like a planetary facility from {argument name="game inspiration" default="No Man's Sky"} but remain original.
title: Planetary Survey Outpost Cutaway
description: Generates a detailed sci-fi cutaway infographic of a planetary survey outpost for concept art, worldbuilding, or game design presentations.

content:
Goal: Create a clean sci-fi technical cutaway diagram of a massive No Man's Sky-inspired space megastructure labeled as {argument name="station name" default="PLANETARY ANCHOR STATION"}, showing a deep-foundation orbital tether/logistics hub in precise industrial detail.

Canvas: Wide 3:2 landscape technical poster on a white background, high-resolution, with generous empty space for labels. Use a realistic hard-surface concept-art style with crisp black leader lines, small circular callout dots, thin technical typography, and subtle shadows under the structure.

Main subject: A huge cross-sectioned vertical space station tilted diagonally from lower left to upper right, with two long horizontal arms extending left and right through the central tower. The structure is blue-gray, gunmetal, white, and dark steel, with exposed copper, orange, green, and black utility piping. The central tower is partially cut away to reveal stacked cylindrical machinery, conduits, elevator shafts, reactor sections, service decks, truss frames, and glowing industrial interiors. The left arm shows many exposed utility pipes inside segmented conduit housing. The right arm is a heavier rectangular radiator/armature with panel plating, circular ports, and mechanical seams. The lower end has a bulky blue anchor base with landing-foot-like supports and shock absorber details.

Top-left text: Large bold uppercase title: {argument name="station name" default="PLANETARY ANCHOR STATION"}. Directly below it, smaller uppercase subtitle: {argument name="diagram subtitle" default="CUTAWAY DIAGRAM"}. Under the subtitle, add a short paragraph: {argument name="description text" default="A deep-foundation megastructure that secures the station to a planetary body and extends high into the atmosphere or low orbit. It functions as a logistics hub, energy collector, and tether anchor."}

Callouts: Include exactly 9 labeled callouts with leader lines and small dot markers, using compact uppercase headings plus one short explanatory sentence each: 1. COMMAND & CONTROL NODE — Navigation, communications, and station management. 2. UPPER TOWER MODULES — Habitation, laboratories, and specialized facilities. 3. DOCKING & MAINTENANCE HUB — Accommodates spacecraft docking, servicing bays, and robotic cranes. 4. STRUCTURAL SPINE — Reinforced central truss that provides strength and alignment for the entire structure. 5. UTILITY CONDUITS — Power cables, coolant pipes, and data lines run through protected conduits. 6. RADIATOR WING — Heat rejection panels with integrated coolant channels. 7. ENERGY CORE — Fusion or high-output power plant providing electricity for the station. 8. ANCHOR BASE — Interface to planetary surface or seabed. Contains anchoring mechanisms and shock absorbers. 9. MAGLEV ELEVATOR SHAFT — Transports personnel and materials along the vertical axis.

Specification box: Place a bordered white box in the lower-right corner titled SPECIFICATIONS. Include exactly 5 rows with small monochrome icons and values: Height (Above Surface) — {argument name="station height" default="~ 6.2 km"}; Span (Arm to Arm) — ~ 7.8 km; Mass — ~ 9.5 million tonnes; Primary Materials — Titanium alloys, Composite ceramics, Reinforced polymers; Power Output — ~ 12.4 GW.

Visual style: Futuristic aerospace engineering diagram, photorealistic 3D model render mixed with infographic annotation, sharp panel lines, exposed mechanical innards, realistic scale, no people, no planets, no starfield, no watermark. Keep the background plain white and the typography clean, black, and highly legible.
title: Planetary Anchor Station Cutaway
description: A detailed sci-fi engineering poster showing a labeled cutaway of a planetary anchor megastructure for worldbuilding or game concept art.

content:
Goal: Create a vertical Japanese awareness poster for {argument name="observance date" default="May 14"}, Thermometer Day, combining a clean educational infographic with a realistic model wearing a themed T-shirt.

Canvas: Tall vertical social-media poster, 9:16 aspect ratio, pale ice-blue background, soft clinical lighting, clean magazine-style layout, blue and red accent palette.

Main subject: On the right half, place a realistic young Japanese woman with a neat chin-length black bob haircut, facing forward from upper thighs up. Her face is intentionally obscured by a plain rectangular blur/mosaic block. She wears a fitted short-sleeve gradient T-shirt fading from sky blue on the left to pink/red on the right. The shirt text reads exactly: “5/14” on the first line, “温度計の日” large below, and “ドイツの物理学者 ファーレンハイトの誕生日” in small text underneath. She holds a white digital thermometer delicately near her shoulder in her right hand.

Top section: On the left, large Japanese headline text reads “5月14日は” and then “温度計の日”, with “温度計” in dark blue and “の日” in red. Add a small line-art thermometer icon above/right of the headline with “°C”. Subtitle below: “— 暮らしを支える、温度のものさし —”. Add a short explanatory paragraph in small Japanese text describing that May 14 is Thermometer Day, related to the German physicist Fahrenheit and the importance of temperature measurement for science, personal life, safety, and comfort.

Decorative icons: Include exactly 3 pale blue scientific/weather icons in the upper-middle left background: 1 vertical thermometer icon, 1 globe icon, and 1 laboratory flask icon.

Middle infographic section: Add a heading “温度計が活躍する場面” with a yellow underline. Beneath it, create exactly 3 rounded rectangular cards in a row, each with a blue outline, a small icon, a bold Japanese title, explanatory text, and a photo thumbnail:
1. Heart icon, title “健康管理に”, text about measuring body temperature and noticing changes early, thumbnail of a digital thermometer on a soft towel showing 36.5.
2. Cooking pot icon, title “料理・暮らしに”, text about cooking temperature control and bath temperature adjustment, thumbnail of a thermometer inserted into a steaming cooking pot.
3. Lab flask icon, title “研究・産業に”, text about accurate temperature measurement supporting technology and products, thumbnail of glass beakers/test tubes with a pipette.

Bottom section: Add a closing heading “温度を知ることは、未来を守ること” in dark blue, a thin horizontal rule, two lines of smaller Japanese explanatory text about climate awareness and protecting life and Earth’s future, and a final red call-to-action line: “今日は温度計に、ちょっと注目してみませんか?” Place a faint green-blue Earth-with-sprout icon at the bottom right.

Visual style: Clean Japanese public-information poster, realistic photography for the woman and card thumbnails, flat minimalist icons, soft shadows, rounded card corners, plenty of white space, professional print layout, no watermark, no extra logos. Use crisp readable Japanese typography and keep the exact visible section count: one top headline section, one model figure, three decorative icons, three application cards, and one bottom message section.
title: Japanese Thermometer Day Poster
description: A vertical Japanese educational poster for Thermometer Day featuring a realistic model, themed T-shirt, infographic cards, and clean science-themed design.

content: Generate a vertical 2:3 high-definition image with the theme 'Architectural Miniature Blueprint'. Building name: {argument name="building_name" default="Nine-Story Demon Tower"}. The frame is a modern architectural design manuscript covered with Chinese floor plans, sections, elevations, structural details, dimensions, and construction notes. A highly realistic miniature model of an ancient Chinese building 'emerges' three-dimensionally from the drawing. The main building should occupy 65%–75% of the frame, providing visual impact. Use a low-angle 3/4 top-down perspective to emphasize height and volume. The building should show a construction state at about 70%–85% completion. Add miniature details: workers, scaffolding, pulleys, materials, carts. The style is a modern architectural concept board + museum-level miniature. Colors are beige, light gray, and black line art. All text must be in Chinese.
title: Architectural Miniature Blueprint Draft
description: A draft-style prompt to generate detailed blueprints and 3D miniature models of Chinese architecture rising from technical drawings.

content:
Use this character to produce a "{argument name="theme" default="weekly fortune omikuji image"}."

Important: The content must be completely different every time. Results should be randomly generated and should not depend on past context or the content of the reference image.

Do not use the reference image as-is; change the situation, composition, expression, pose, background, props, and layout design to match this specific fortune result.

For the overall fortune, use standard luck rankings found in Japanese omikuji culture.

Place the overall fortune result prominently at the top of the screen.

The text design should be fun and information-rich, mixing elements of traditional Japanese fortunes, magazine horoscopes, variety shows, social media images, and internet memes.

The character should speak their reaction or thoughts on the fortune result using speech bubbles.

The content of the speech bubbles, facial expressions, energy levels, and reactions must change every time.

Include multiple fortune categories.

For each category, add a small cut-out illustration of a "yuru-fuwa" (soft/cuddly) chibi-style character.

The fortune categories, content, layout, color scheme, font styles, and amount of information must change every time.

Include "X (formerly Twitter) luck" as well.

Overall Atmosphere:

- {argument name="atmosphere" default="Cute, soft/fluffy, slightly quirky"} - Something people want to share on social media - Feels like a horoscope page at the back of a magazine - Heisei to Reiwa internet culture - High information density - Looks great in screenshots

Important: Significantly vary the fortune content, text, chibi character actions, layout, specific jokes, and overall vibe every time.

Do not repeat the same pattern.

Negative Elements:
Avoid accurate dates, days of the week, calendar notations, real-world time information, specific schedules, incorrect date generation, broken number representations, nonsensical date text, or unreadable calendar UI.
title: Fortune Telling Omikuji Card Generator
description: A detailed instruction set for creating stylized, information-rich fortune-telling images using a reference character, blending modern internet culture with traditional Japanese aesthetics.

content:
Goal: Create a clean, high-end Japanese B2B SaaS pitch deck / service concept board for {argument name="service name" default="NOVALITH"}, showing exactly 9 widescreen slides arranged in a 3 by 3 grid as if previewing an LP, mobile top, banner, and presentation materials.

Canvas: One landscape image containing 9 separate 16:9 slide thumbnails, evenly spaced with thin pale borders. White background, navy and teal corporate palette, light cyan diagonal line accents, subtle geometric dots, and a teal triangular corner ribbon on the upper-left of every slide containing the slide number 1 through 9 in white.

Visual style: Modern Japanese startup / enterprise SaaS design, polished vector UI, minimal gradients, soft shadows, rounded cards, thin teal data lines, navy headings, plenty of whitespace. Use a stylized geometric N logo in teal and navy, with spaced uppercase wordmark {argument name="service name" default="NOVALITH"}. No people or photos; use only icons, charts, dashboards, and abstract product visuals.

Text language: All visible slide copy should be in Japanese. Use crisp, presentation-ready typography with bold navy headlines and smaller explanatory subtext.

Slide count and layout details: Include exactly 9 slides:
1. Cover slide. Large NOVALITH logo centered above the headline 「データを、未来の意思決定に変える。」 and subheading 「中小企業のためのデータ予測プラットフォーム」. Decorative teal diagonal accents in the corners.
2. Data integration slide. Headline 「すべてのデータをひとつに統合」 with short subtext about automatically collecting and integrating scattered data. Center shows a stacked isometric database/platform cube with the N logo on top. Four connected input/output cards: 「データベース」, 「CSV / ファイル」, 「SaaS」, 「API」.
3. Forecasting slide. Headline 「高精度な予測で未来を先読み」. Large line chart card titled 「売上予測（12ヶ月）」 with legend 「実績」 and 「予測」, months along the x-axis, an upward teal trend line, and a floating KPI card reading 「予測値（来期）¥28,450,000」.
4. Risk detection slide. Headline 「リスクを事前に検知し迅速に対応」. Left chart card titled 「リスクスコアの推移」 showing a declining zigzag teal line with dates 4/1 to 4/29 and y-axis levels 高, 中, 低. Add a warning callout 「リスク上昇を検知」. Right side has exactly 3 feature rows with circular icons: anomaly detection, alert notification, and rapid response.
5. Dashboard slide. Headline 「ダッシュボードで全体を見える化」. Show a full SaaS dashboard screenshot: dark navy left sidebar with logo and exactly 6 menu items, and a main area with KPI cards, a donut chart, and a sales forecast line chart. Visible KPI examples include 「売上予測（今月）¥28,450,000」, 「需要予測（今月）15,782」, and 「リスクスコア 24/100」.
6. Automated report slide. Headline 「レポートを自動生成し共有もかんたん」. Center a white report card titled 「月次サマリーレポート」 with bullet points showing positive percentages such as +18.6% and +12.4%, a NOVALITH logo in the corner, a teal bar chart on the right, and a teal button labeled 「レポートをダウンロード」.
7. Security slide. Headline 「セキュアな環境で安心して利用」. Central shield-and-padlock illustration with floating security particles. Bottom has exactly 3 security feature icons and labels: 「暗号化通信（SSL/TLS）」, 「アクセス制御（権限分割）」, and 「定期バックアップ（自動）」.
8. Simple steps slide. Headline 「シンプルなステップですぐに始められる」. Show exactly 4 horizontally connected step cards with dotted arrows: step 1 database icon 「データを接続」, step 2 node/network icon 「モデルを選択」, step 3 play icon 「予測を実行」, step 4 bar-chart icon 「結果を確認・活用」. Each card includes a short explanatory line underneath.
9. Closing CTA slide. Headline 「さあ、データの力で未来をつくりましょう」 with subtext 「NOVALITHが、あなたのビジネスの成長をデータで支えます。」. Large centered logo and wordmark. Bottom has exactly 2 call-to-action buttons: filled teal button 「無料で試してみる　→」 and outlined button 「資料をダウンロード　↓」.

Constraints: Maintain consistent brand colors, logo, spacing, slide numbering, and diagonal teal corner motif across all 9 slides. The overall image should look like a single polished overview sheet of presentation thumbnails, not isolated full-screen slides. Avoid extra slides, extra buttons, stock photos, watermarks, or unrelated text.
title: NOVALITH SaaS Pitch Deck Grid
description: Generates a polished 3x3 Japanese B2B SaaS presentation overview for a fictional data prediction platform.

content:
Goal: Create a highly realistic physical museum-diorama style city map titled {argument name="archive title" default="TOKYO — NOCTURNAL URBAN ARCHIVE"}, showing Tokyo at night as a layered architectural archive model on a dark tabletop.

Canvas: Wide landscape image, 3:2 aspect ratio, viewed from an elevated oblique angle. The entire scene is a rectangular black archival board with a thin cream double-line border, coordinate grid, index markings, and printed cartographic annotations. Use a restrained night palette: charcoal paper, navy water, warm amber city lights, cool white road lines, and selective blue/purple tower lighting.

Layout: The main map occupies the upper 80% of the board and shows an ultra-dense 3D miniature urban model with thousands of low-rise buildings, raised clusters of skyscrapers, glowing roads, rail lines, bridges, waterways, parks, and harbor piers. The bottom 20% is a printed archive information strip containing a legend on the left, the title block in the center, a scale bar, a north arrow, and an index grid on the right. Add realistic paper grain, matte model surfaces, tiny cast shadows, engraved-looking map lines, and soft studio lighting from above.

City content: Depict {argument name="city name" default="Tokyo"} as a nocturnal urban archive. Include exactly 9 labeled landmark callout plaques on the map, each black with thin cream borders and bilingual Japanese/English styling: 1) Shinjuku, 2) Shibuya, 3) Imperial Palace, 4) Ueno, 5) Asakusa, 6) Tokyo Skytree, 7) Ginza, 8) Odaiba, 9) Tokyo Bay. Place Shinjuku as a dense blue-lit skyscraper cluster on the left, Shibuya lower-left, the dark green Imperial Palace park in the center, Tokyo Tower glowing orange near the center-lower area, Tokyo Skytree glowing blue-purple on the right, Ginza near the waterfront, Odaiba on an island near the bottom, and Tokyo Bay as dark navy water on the right.

Map details: Add a coordinate frame with columns numbered 1 through 10 across the top and rows labeled A through E down the left edge. Include latitude/longitude markings around the border. Show a dense street network as fine pale lines, major expressways as brighter sweeping curves, rail lines as subtly colored routes, and water areas as smooth dark navy shapes. Include a prominent illuminated suspension bridge across Tokyo Bay in the lower-right quadrant.

Bottom archive strip: Include exactly 1 legend box on the bottom-left with the heading “Legend” and 8 line/style entries: 1) Yamanote Line, 2) JR / Chuo Line, 3) Ginza Line, 4) Asakusa Line, 5) Yurikamome Line, 6) Water, 7) Major Road, 8) Park / Green. In the center title block, print “TOKYO — NOCTURNAL URBAN ARCHIVE”, a smaller subtitle “Landscape Archive Model”, and an archive code {argument name="archive code" default="TKY-NAM-2024-001"}. Include exactly 1 scale bar labeled “Scale 1:25,000” with tick labels from 0 to 2.5 km. Include exactly 1 north arrow and exactly 1 index grid at the far right with 25 cells labeled A1 through E5.

Visual style: Photorealistic macro photography of a handcrafted physical cartographic diorama, like an architectural museum model photographed in a dark studio. Use crisp micro-detail, shallow-but-readable depth of field, realistic shadows under elevated buildings, reflective water, warm window glows, and subtle paper texture. No people, no cars large enough to dominate, no extra landmarks beyond the specified labels, no watermark.
title: Tokyo Nocturnal Urban Archive
description: A structured prompt for generating a photorealistic nighttime Tokyo architectural archive diorama with labeled landmarks, map legend, scale bar, and index grid.

content:
Goal: Create a museum-quality physical diorama map titled {argument name="map title" default="PARIS — NOCTURNAL URBAN ARCHIVE"}, showing {argument name="city" default="Paris"} at night as an archival landscape model with glowing streets, raised landmarks, annotation labels, and a refined cartographic border.

Canvas: Landscape 3:2 perspective view of a thick rectangular archive board resting on a dark matte tabletop. Camera is angled from above, slightly front-facing, so the board has visible thickness and cast shadows. Use a dark navy, charcoal, and antique-gold palette with restrained warm amber lighting.

Layout: The main map fills most of the board, surrounded by a double-line gold border and an alphanumeric coordinate grid. Use letters A–K across the top edge and numbers 1–8 down the side edges. At the bottom, include a structured information band split into 5 visible panels: 1) a legend panel, 2) a compass rose panel, 3) a large centered title/archive text panel, 4) a scale bar panel, and 5) a locator-map panel.

Subject details: Render the city as a dense low-relief 3D urban model with thousands of tiny matte buildings, engraved street blocks, and a dark blue river cutting horizontally through the lower-middle area. Major boulevards and bridges glow as fine amber light lines, creating a precise nighttime network. The water should be satin navy with subtle reflections. Parks should appear as darker textured green-black areas.

Raised landmarks: Include exactly 4 prominent illuminated miniature landmarks, each larger and more sculptural than the surrounding buildings: 1) the Eiffel Tower in the lower-left/left-center area near the river, glowing gold; 2) the Arc de Triomphe in the upper-left area with bright radial avenues; 3) Notre-Dame Cathedral on the island near the center-right river bend; 4) Sacré-Cœur on the upper-right hill area. Keep the landmarks physically plausible, matte, finely detailed, and warmly lit.

Map labels and annotations: Add fine serif gold typography directly on the map, like an architectural archive plate. Include exactly 14 visible district/area labels: XVII Batignolles Monceau, VIII Élysée, IX Opéra, XIII Montmartre, XIX La Villette, X Entrepôt, II Bourse, III Temple, IV Hôtel de Ville, XI Bastille, XII Reuilly, XIII Gobelins, VI Luxembourg, XIV Montparnasse. Add major route labels including Champs-Élysées, Boulevard Haussmann, Boulevard Saint-Germain, and Boulevard Bessières. Labels should be legible but small, integrated into the model.

Bottom information band text: In the center title panel, show the title {argument name="map title" default="PARIS — NOCTURNAL URBAN ARCHIVE"}. Under it, add the subtitle {argument name="subtitle" default="CITY OF LIGHTS · HISTORIC CORE · HAUSSMANN NETWORK"}. Add a final small line: {argument name="archive code" default="ARCHIVE CODE: PAR-NAM-2024-001"}. In the legend panel, list exactly 5 items with small symbols: Monument, Major Axis, Boulevard, Bridge, Park / Garden, Water. In the scale panel, show “SCALE 1:10,000” with a segmented bar labeled 0, 100, 200, 300, 400, 500, and 1000 M. In the locator panel, include a small grid diagram and a tiny outline map labeled “CENTRAL PARIS FRANCE.”

Visual style: Hyper-realistic macro photography of a handcrafted architectural archive model; matte black paper, engraved gold ink, miniature plaster or resin buildings, warm pinprick LED-like city lights, shallow depth of field, crisp central details, soft vignette, realistic paper grain, tactile board edges, subtle dust and material texture. The image should feel like a physical museum diorama photographed in low light, not a flat digital map.

Constraints: Keep the palette dark and elegant; no people, cars, modern UI, bright neon, or glossy plastic. Preserve the single-board archival presentation and the exact 4 raised landmarks, 5 bottom panels, 5 legend items, and 14 district/area labels.
title: Paris Nocturnal Urban Archive Map
description: Generates a cinematic physical diorama-style night map of Paris with raised landmarks, glowing boulevards, archival labels, and museum cartography details.

content:
Goal: Create a highly detailed physical “Landscape Archive Model” museum diorama of {argument name="city name" default="New York City"} at night, presented as an architectural archive board with a raised 3D city model integrated into a flat cartographic map.

Canvas: Wide horizontal 16:9 image, photographed from an oblique overhead angle like a premium editorial product shot. The board sits on a dark surface with subtle shadows, a thick matte edge, and tactile paper/cardboard texture. Use a restrained night palette of deep navy, charcoal blue, muted brass, warm amber window lights, cream annotation lines, and dark green park areas.

Layout: The entire image is one large rectangular archive plate with a thin double-line border, coordinate grid letters along the top and bottom, coordinate numbers along both sides, and faint technical map grid lines. The central area shows Manhattan as a dense raised miniature city, surrounded by darker flat-map boroughs and waterways. The 3D buildings should look like matte architectural model blocks with tiny lit windows and cast shadows. The waterways are dark blue, slightly satin, and labeled directly on the map.

Main title block: Place a boxed title panel in the upper left reading exactly “NEW YORK CITY”, with smaller lines “NOCTURNAL URBAN ARCHIVE” and “NYC-NAM-2024-001”. Add tiny archive text above it such as “LANDSCAPE ARCHIVE MODEL / 地景档案模型”. Add a small top-right study label reading “EAST RIVER CORRIDOR STUDY” and “EDITION 01”.

Subject details: Show Manhattan as the main raised island, packed with hundreds of small buildings and warm light points. Include a tall glowing Empire State Building near midtown, a bright cluster around Times Square, One World Trade Center near lower Manhattan, the Chrysler Building on the east side, Brooklyn Bridge crossing the East River, and a rectangular dark Central Park inset near the top-center of Manhattan. Surrounding flat-map areas should include New Jersey on the left, Queens on the right, and Brooklyn in the lower right.

Counted visible map labels: Include exactly 15 prominent callouts/region labels: 1) “NEW JERSEY / HUDSON COUNTY”, 2) “HUDSON RIVER”, 3) “ONE WORLD TRADE CENTER / 1,776 ft / 541 m”, 4) “TIMES SQUARE / THEATER DISTRICT”, 5) “EMPIRE STATE BUILDING / 1,454 ft / 443 m”, 6) “CENTRAL PARK”, 7) “CHRYSLER BUILDING / 1,046 ft / 319 m”, 8) “EAST RIVER”, 9) “BROOKLYN BRIDGE / 1,595 ft / 486 m”, 10) “QUEENS / LONG ISLAND CITY”, 11) “BROOKLYN / DOWNTOWN BROOKLYN”, 12) “LEGEND”, 13) “NORTH”, 14) “SCALE 1:25,000”, 15) “ARCHIVE NOTES”. Use thin cream leader lines and small boxed annotation plates for landmark callouts.

Legend and information panels: In the lower left, create one boxed legend with exactly 6 legend entries: “PRIMARY ROAD”, “SECONDARY ROAD”, “RAIL LINE”, “WATER BODY”, “PARK / GREEN SPACE”, and “MAJOR LANDMARK / BUILDING FOOTPRINT”. Below or near it, include one small north arrow compass and one scale bar labeled in miles and kilometers. In the lower right, create one archive notes box with a paragraph of tiny technical text plus exactly 4 metadata fields: “ARCHIVE CODE NYC-NAM-2024-001”, “MODEL NO. 001”, “SHEET NO. 01 / 01”, and data-source text in very small print.

Visual style: Ultra-realistic miniature architectural model photography mixed with precise cartographic design, museum archive aesthetic, sharp details, physical paper grain, slightly worn board edges, amber pinprick lights, clean serif title typography, tiny monospaced technical labels, and dramatic but soft nocturnal studio lighting.

Constraints: Keep the image as a single finished physical archive board, not a digital UI. Maintain exact visible landmark hierarchy and the listed label count. No people, vehicles, logos, watermark, or extra decorative text. Preserve the nighttime restrained color palette and the tactile model-map hybrid look.
title: New York Night Archive Diorama
description: Generates a museum-style nocturnal architectural archive board showing New York City as a raised miniature map diorama with precise landmark annotations.

content:
Goal: Create a cinematic physical museum-diorama map titled {argument name="city archive title" default="LONDON — NOCTURNAL URBAN ARCHIVE"}, showing {argument name="city" default="London"} at night as a raised 3D architectural archive model on a flat printed cartographic board.

Canvas: Wide horizontal 16:9 image, three-quarter overhead view, dark studio background, the board seen in perspective like a premium museum exhibit or archival planning model. Use a restrained nocturnal palette: deep blue-black paper, navy water, charcoal city blocks, warm amber streetlights, muted gold linework, and subtle cool highlights.

Main diorama: A detailed miniature city map with raised matte buildings, parks, bridges, and river geometry. The River Thames snakes across the center in dark blue, labeled “RIVER THAMES.” Dense street grids glow with tiny amber lights. Major landmarks are slightly taller, more sculptural, and individually illuminated, with thin vertical annotation leader lines rising to labels.

Visible landmark annotations: Include exactly 6 large top-edge labels with leader lines: “BUCKINGHAM PALACE,” “BIG BEN / PALACE OF WESTMINSTER,” “ST. PAUL’S CATHEDRAL,” “THE SHARD,” “THE GHERKIN,” and “CANARY WHARF.” Also include smaller map labels for these 14 places: Regent’s Park, Paddington, Marylebone, Oxford Street, Hyde Park, Green Park, St. James’s Park, Buckingham Palace, Victoria, Westminster, City of London, Southwark, Bermondsey, and Greenwich.

Key raised structures: Emphasize exactly 7 landmark model groups: Buckingham Palace at lower left with glowing frontage, Palace of Westminster and Big Ben beside the river, St. Paul’s Cathedral with a domed roof, The Shard as a bright triangular glass tower, The Gherkin as a dark bullet-shaped tower, Tower Bridge crossing the river in warm light, and Canary Wharf as a cluster of tall golden-lit towers at upper right.

Map and border system: Add a precise archival grid overlay with letters along the top edge and numbers along the side edges, fine gold coordinate lines, double-line border, and thin annotation ticks. Keep the paper surface tactile with visible grain, slight bevel thickness, and realistic cast shadows from raised buildings and label lines.

Bottom information band: Create exactly 5 boxed panels along the bottom. Panel 1 is a legend with exactly 6 icon rows: Major Landmark, Historic Building, Park / Green Space, Water Body, Bridge, Railway Station. Panel 2 is a narrow empty divider/label strip. Panel 3 is the central title block with the title, a subtitle reading “LANDSCAPE ARCHIVE MODEL / 地景檔案模型,” an archive code reading {argument name="archive code" default="LDN-NAM-2024-001"}, and a dual scale bar in miles and kilometers. Panel 4 contains a decorative compass rose with N/E/S/W markings. Panel 5 contains an “INDEX GRID” mini-map and a small coordinate-system note reading “COORDINATE SYSTEM / BRITISH NATIONAL GRID / TRANSVERSE MERCATOR / OSGB 1936.”

Lighting and material style: Photorealistic editorial render, macro miniature depth, museum-grade craftsmanship, warm amber city illumination against cool blue shadows, matte paper and painted resin buildings, crisp gold typography, shallow atmospheric vignette, no people, no vehicles except tiny indistinct boats on the river, no watermark.

Constraints: Keep the composition consistent and legible as a single physical archive board. Use exact counts for the 6 large top labels, 14 smaller place labels, 7 raised landmark groups, 5 bottom panels, and 6 legend rows. Preserve the archival cartographic aesthetic rather than a modern digital UI.
title: London Nocturnal Urban Archive Diorama
description: A detailed prompt for generating a museum-style nocturnal 3D cartographic diorama of London with landmarks, annotations, legend panels, and archival map styling.

content:
Goal: Create a cute nursery-style Japanese hiragana learning poster on a light wood wall, as if generated from text only, with a slightly imperfect AI-made alphabet chart look.

Canvas: Square 1:1 image, softly lit by daylight from a window on the left. The poster is an off-white sheet pinned to the wall with four small round wooden tacks, one near each corner. Include a small potted plant and low bookshelf partially visible at the lower left for a cozy classroom or child room setting.

Main subject: A large hiragana chart poster titled {argument name="headline text" default="あいうえおひょう"} in rounded pink-red lettering with a white outline at the top center. Below the title, make a neat grid of exactly 63 rounded rectangular tiles, arranged as 9 rows by 7 columns. Each tile contains one large dark navy handwritten-style kana or punctuation mark, sometimes with tiny superscript-like numbers, dakuten marks, or small decorative icons. The text should look charming but not fully correct, like an AI-generated hiragana table.

Grid content: Use exactly 63 visible tiles. Row 1: あ, へ, う, よ, ー with tiny blue fish, う, あ with tiny apple. Row 2: と, ば, う, て, い with tiny apple, う, bunny icon. Row 3: い, ん with small 6, え, い with tiny yellow fruit, お with small 1, い with tiny apple, う with tiny orange. Row 4: お, い with comma, お with tiny green grapes, り with tiny apple, ま, す with tiny rabbit face, ば with small 6. Row 5: じ with tiny apple, い with tiny yellow chick face, さ with tiny pink fruit, い with small 2, あ with small 2, う with tiny green grapes, あ with small 2. Row 6: て with comma, よ with small 3, ち with tiny chick face, と with tiny green grapes, 々, か with tiny yellow fruit, が with tiny orange. Row 7: も with exclamation, い with tiny chick face, あ with small 2, よ, う with tiny chick face, ひ with tiny apple, も with small 2. Row 8: お with small 3, と, ら with small 3, え, ! with tiny blue fish, す with small 3, ! with tiny squirrel icon. Row 9: あ with tiny apple, う with tiny apple, て with tiny cat icon, ら with tiny orange, お with small 1, お with tiny f-like mark, ひ with tiny yellow chick.

Visual style: Soft warm pastel palette, clean rounded poster design, child-friendly educational wall chart, subtle paper texture, thin pale beige tile borders, dark blue brush-calligraphy kana, small kawaii fruit and animal stickers. Use {argument name="wall material" default="pale vertical wood paneling"}, {argument name="poster paper color" default="warm ivory"}, and {argument name="letter color" default="deep navy blue"}.

Constraints: Keep the image square, front-facing with slight natural perspective, no watermark, no extra text beyond the title and grid characters, and keep the poster readable while retaining the whimsical imperfect AI-generated kana-chart quality.
title: Cute Hiragana Chart Poster
description: Generates a cozy square image of a nursery-style hiragana learning poster with a 63-tile kana grid, kawaii icons, and warm wooden room decor.

title: AI Nike-chan Drawing Song Manga

content:
Goal: Create a clean vector icon set for business presentation illustrations, suitable for slide decks and documents.

Canvas: Wide 16:9 white background, evenly spaced grid layout with 40 discrete icons arranged in 5 rows and 8 columns. Use generous margins and consistent icon sizing.

Visual style: Flat modern corporate vector style with rounded shapes, minimal shading, crisp edges, and a friendly SaaS/business look. Use a limited palette of {argument name="primary blue" default="bright blue"}, {argument name="accent green" default="fresh green"}, {argument name="accent orange" default="vivid orange"}, dark navy outlines, light gray UI details, and occasional yellow. No text labels, no watermark, no realistic texture.

Icon contents: Include exactly 40 icons in this order, left to right by row:
Row 1: 1) three people meeting at a table with speech bubbles, 2) bar chart with upward growth arrow, 3) line graph with three colored points and rising arrow, 4) three vertical upward arrows in blue green orange, 5) presentation screen with pie chart and pointer, 6) laptop with dashboard cards, 7) tablet with colored dashboard blocks.
Row 2: 8) kanban board with three columns and colored task cards, 9) glowing light bulb idea icon, 10) target with arrow hitting bullseye, 11) handshake with blue and green sleeves, 12) monthly calendar grid with one orange date, 13) document page with blue folded corner and lines, 14) checklist document with green check marks.
Row 3: 15) cloud upload icon with upward arrow, 16) dark blue gear settings icon, 17) magnifying glass search icon, 18) blue envelope email icon, 19) video conference monitor with four participants, 20) gold trophy award icon, 21) four-piece puzzle in blue green orange.
Row 4: 22) green circle check mark, 23) pie chart in blue green orange, 24) organization chart with connected colored nodes, 25) thumbs-up approval icon, 26) team/group icon with three people, 27) bar chart with rising green arrow, 28) green shield with white check mark.
Row 5: 29) money bag with dollar sign and stacked coins, 30) calculator with orange key, 31) analog clock, 32) yellow file folder, 33) overlapping chat bubbles with ellipsis, 34) dark blue briefcase with yellow clasp, 35) blue globe wireframe.

content: Create a clean technical system diagram of a residential {argument name="system type" default="greywater recycling system"} for toilet flushing. Show {argument name="sources" default="three water sources"} on the left: kitchen vegetable washing sink, bathroom wash basin, and washing machine. Each source connects through a diverter valve to either the original sewer drain or the greywater recycling line. The greywater line goes into a pre-filter module with coarse mesh filter, sediment trap, washable cartridge filter, and grease separator for kitchen water. Then show a low-level greywater collection tank with overflow pipe to sewer, high and low water level sensors, and inspection cover. From this tank, show a greywater transfer pump sending water upward into a large white plastic storage tank. The large tank has low, middle, and high water level sensors, overflow to sewer, drain outlet, optional UV disinfection module, and ventilation. From the storage tank, show a pump or gravity outlet feeding the toilet cistern. Include a backup clean water supply with air gap and backflow prevention to the toilet cistern. Show a {argument name="controller" default="Raspberry Pi 4 controller"} connected with signal lines to sensors, solenoid valves, pumps, leak sensor, and alarm. Use blue arrows for water flow, red arrows for overflow/emergency drain, dotted black lines for electronic control. Style: modern engineering schematic, white background, labeled components, clear arrows, minimal but detailed.
title: Residential Greywater Recycling Schematic
description: A detailed engineering diagram prompt for a residential greywater recycling system, showing components like filters, tanks, pumps, and a Raspberry Pi controller with clear flow indicators.

content: Create a square black-and-white puzzle poster in the style of an extremely intricate hand-drawn notebook maze, clean monochrome ink on white paper. At the top center, place a bold Japanese headline reading {argument name="headline text" default="この問題、あなたは正解できますか？"} in a Mincho-style serif font. Below it, fill almost the entire page with one huge rectangular maze inside a thin double-line border, made from thousands of tiny orthogonal corridors, micro-grid strokes, dead ends, and tight right-angle passages, like a 2026 upgraded version of a middle-school original maze. The maze should be overwhelmingly dense and difficult, with no color and no shading except black linework. Include exactly 15 larger embedded maze motifs visible within the dense field: 4 concentric square spiral targets, 3 circular spiral targets, 2 large X-shaped starburst maze structures, 1 tall rectangular diamond/chevron maze block, 1 central nested-square pyramid-like maze, 1 large asymmetric diamond spiral maze in the lower-left center, 1 large diagonal crossing X near the lower center, and 2 horizontal ladder/grid maze blocks. Add faint long straight divider-like corridors running vertically and horizontally through the maze, plus diagonal corridor cuts, while keeping every area packed with tiny solvable-looking paths. Overall composition: crisp scan-like puzzle sheet, centered, high contrast, no illustrations, no characters, no gradients, no background texture beyond the maze lines.
title: Ultra-Dense Japanese Maze Puzzle
description: Generates a challenging monochrome Japanese maze poster suitable for puzzle contests, social media challenges, or printable brain teasers.

content:
Using this character, draw {argument name="theme" default="a page from a 1970s-era children's book about the occult, future science, or paranormal phenomena"}.

Do not use the reference image as is; create a situation and composition that suits the content of the page.

Important: The content should be interpreted by a 1970s children's book editor who forcibly links the reference image to paranormal phenomena or future science.

Make the page look as if an adult at the time saw this character, assumed it was a meaningful entity, and developed strange theories, super-logic, or occult interpretations.

Focus on leaps of logic that seem plausible yet are nonsensical.

The content should be random each time, with the editor's delusions and assertions running wild. Include assertive text designed to provoke children's curiosity.

The layout should be in the style of a 1970s children's book. Include old paper textures, misaligned printing, fading, crude illustrations, red and blue ink, hand-drawn arrows, and nonsensical charts.

Each time:
- Different far-fetched interpretations
- Different page layouts
- Different assertions
- Different strange theories
- Different camera angles

Important: Prioritize the feeling of a 'strange children's book that might have actually existed.' Instead of orthodox sci-fi, prioritize the enthusiasm of an occult editor with strange theories, forced connections, and leaps of logic.
title: 1970s Occult Children's Book Page
description: A creative prompt designed for image-to-image generation that transforms a character into a 1970s-style Japanese children's book page, complete with retro printing artifacts and pseudo-scientific occult explanations.

content:
Goal: Create a dreamy anime “charm point” visual analysis sheet that explains why a cute summer girl is lovable, presented like a scrapbook character appeal diagram rather than a standard profile.

Canvas: Vertical 3:4 portrait poster on an off-white paper background with thin pastel blue and pink border lines, rounded inset panels, dotted arrows, tiny hearts, sparkles, seashell doodles, handwritten notes, and soft stationery-like annotations.

Main subject: Center a delicate anime girl named {argument name="character name" default="Sunny Days with You"} sitting by a bright seaside. She has {argument name="hair color" default="soft pink-blonde"} long flowing hair with wispy bangs, large aqua-blue eyes, fair skin with gentle blush, and a shy slightly parted-lips expression. Her pose is three-quarter view, looking back over her shoulder, one hand lightly touching her lips and chin, with elegant fingers. She wears a white ruffled summer dress with sleeveless frilled straps, layered skirt, and navy blue ribbon bows at the chest and waist. Add an oversized white sun hat decorated with a large navy-blue bow, a sunflower, and ribbon tails. The background behind her is a bright blue ocean, white waves, blue sky, and fluffy clouds.

Layout and counted detail panels: Use exactly 9 inset panels around the central character, each with rounded corners and soft pastel borders. Left column has 4 labeled panels: 1) “eyes” close-up showing both sparkling aqua eyes and eyelashes, 2) “hair flow” close-up of pink hair strands blowing in the sea breeze, 3) “gesture” close-up of crossed delicate fingers, 4) “soft expression” vertical panel containing exactly 4 small stacked facial-expression crops: gentle gaze, shy glance, pouting/soft look, and smiling with closed eyes. Right side has 4 labeled panels: 5) “favorite detail” close-up of the navy bow and sunflower on the hat, 6) “best angle” profile/three-quarter face crop from the cutest side angle, 7) “outfit detail” close-up of the ruffled shoulder strap and bodice, 8) “summer motif” collage panel with exactly 7 small motif items: three seashells, one conch shell, one blue ribbon bow, one circular ocean-sky scene, and one tiny sparkle/flower cluster. Bottom center has 1 labeled panel: 9) “tiny habit” close-up of her lower face and hand resting near the chin.

Text content: Top-left handwritten headline reads {argument name="headline text" default="charm point.♡"} in pink cursive, with the small subtitle “SUNNY DAYS WITH YOU” beneath it. Include the labels “eyes”, “hair flow”, “gesture”, “soft expression”, “favorite detail”, “best angle”, “outfit detail”, “summer motif”, and “tiny habit”. Add small handwritten accent phrases: “My favorite” at the top right, “So cute! ♡” near the best-angle area, “chin rest...♡” below the tiny-habit panel, and {argument name="bottom quote" default="You make every day shine."} near the bottom left.

Visual style: High-quality Japanese anime illustration, pastel watercolor and colored-pencil finish, airy summer lighting, glossy eyes, soft blush, detailed hair strands, luminous sea reflections, delicate line art, cute handwritten typography, scrapbook annotation aesthetic, romantic and affectionate mood.

Constraints: Keep the central girl dominant and full-body/waist-up within the page, keep all 9 inset panels visible and clearly separated, use only soft pastel colors with navy accents, avoid photorealism, avoid extra characters, avoid watermark or logo.
title: Anime Charm Point Analysis Sheet
description: Generates a pastel anime scrapbook-style character appeal diagram highlighting lovable details, expressions, outfit features, and summer motifs.

content:
Create a square 1:1 comic-style meme infographic titled {argument name="headline text" default="What is a World Model?"} in huge bold black lettering centered at the top on a white background. Below the title, draw a clean black-outlined 3-column by 3-row grid with an extra left label column, like a humorous taxonomy chart. Column headers across the top are exactly three: “Predicts”, “Simulates”, and “Breaks Your Brain”. Row labels down the left are exactly three: “Robots”, “Foundation Models”, and “Movie Night”. Use thick black grid lines, white cell backgrounds, and colorful hand-drawn cartoon illustrations with crisp outlines, resembling playful science/AI memes.

Cell 1, Robots × Predicts: title “DreamerV3”. Show a cute small robot sleeping on a blue pillow/blanket, with “Zzz” nearby and a thought bubble containing three small sequential robot/world prediction panels ending with a red flag.

Cell 2, Robots × Simulates: title “Tiny Sandbox”. Show a glass snow-globe terrarium on a wooden base with a tiny robot standing on grass beside a tree, clouds, birds, and a sun inside the dome.

Cell 3, Robots × Breaks Your Brain: title “Reward Goblin”. Show a small robot startled by a frantic green goblin chasing floating gold star rewards along a dotted path, with a red exclamation mark near the robot.

Cell 4, Foundation Models × Predicts: title “Autocomplete Earth”. Show a retro computer-like AI with a green smiling screen and robot arms hovering over a globe, with a thought bubble containing a weather icon, a newspaper labeled “NEWS”, and a smiling emoji.

Cell 5, Foundation Models × Simulates: title “Weather Wizard”. Show an old wizard in a blue robe and pointed hat casting spells over a world map or globe-like table, surrounded by weather icons: storm cloud with lightning, grey cloud, rain cloud, tornado, and bright sun, plus a small server rack at the side.

Cell 6, Foundation Models × Breaks Your Brain: title “Everything Machine”. Show a giant black top-hat-shaped machine labeled {argument name="machine label" default="FM"} with a flexible hose/tube, generating many outputs: a cloud, purple DNA strand, red car, small robot, and a document page.

Cell 7, Movie Night × Predicts: title “The Truman Show”. Show a man standing inside a transparent dome on a circular stage with a suburban house, fence, grass, clouds, and city skyline inside, surrounded by multiple studio cameras and spotlights outside the dome.

Cell 8, Movie Night × Simulates: title “The Matrix”. Show a person seated in a futuristic chair wearing a black outfit, plugged into cables, against falling green code, flanked by one red pill and one blue pill. Keep the face simplified or obscured.

Cell 9, Movie Night × Breaks Your Brain: title “SimCity God Mode”. Show a giant hand descending from a cloud and placing or pinching a building above a miniature city with roads, houses, factories, smoke, small people looking up with “?!” speech bubbles, and a bright sky.

Constraints: include exactly 9 illustrated content cells, exactly 3 column headers, exactly 3 row labels, and exactly the cell titles listed above. Keep all typography bold, legible, black, and meme-like. Use a polished cartoon infographic style, not photorealistic. No watermark, no extra logos, no additional panels.
title: World Model Meme Grid
description: A square comic infographic explaining world models through nine humorous AI and movie-inspired meme panels.

content:
Please generate a high-quality "Urban Line Atlas Poster."

This image is designed based on the visual language of a "monochrome urban line-drawing travel poster," featuring a complete composition with a large top title, mid-view street depth, core landmarks, flanking buildings, foreground figures, and a bottom signature block.

[Theme City]
City Name: {argument name="city" default="Yichun"}
English Main Title: {argument name="English title" default="FOREST LINE ATLAS / YICHUN CHRONICLE"}
Chinese Title: Yichun · City of Forests
Country/Region: Heilongjiang Province, China
Core Landmarks: Lesser Khingan Mountains forest, Korean pine forest sea, Wuying National Forest Park observation tower
Street Scene: Small forest town streets, residential intersections with wooden buildings, forestry city atmosphere
Emotional Theme: Commuting moments in the morning mist
Main Color Tone: {argument name="main color" default="dark green + dark grey line drawing"}
Aspect Ratio: 3:4 vertical

[Overall Positioning]
This is a work that serves as a travel souvenir poster, an urban visual guide, an architectural line manuscript, and an editorial design cover. The image needs to present Yichun's unique temperament as a "city in the forest," with both urban recognition and a sense of real street life—not a standard travel poster or a single landmark illustration, but a visual archive piece of collectible value.
[Composition Design]
The top is a strong title area using the large English title "FOREST LINE ATLAS," supplemented by the subtitle "YICHUN CHRONICLE." A numbering system (e.g., No. CN-HLJ-YC-01) and brief coordinate info can be added, with a modern and design-oriented layout.
The middle is the urban street主体, using a deep street perspective composition with a slightly low angle, the road extending into the distance and gradually entering the forest area.
The core landmark is the forest observation tower or the silhouette of the Lesser Khingan forest in the distance, clear but not overly prominent, naturally blending into the street scene.
Add local architectural details on both sides: wooden houses, forestry bureau-style buildings, residential blocks, small shops, bus stops, streetlights, utility poles, simple road signs, etc., forming high-density urban information.
The foreground includes lifestyle elements: pedestrians in autumn/winter clothing, people on bicycles, residents with shopping bags, small vehicles, and fallen leaves on the ground to enhance the seasonal atmosphere.
A separate signature area at the bottom is designed as a city archive label structure, containing "YICHUN / HEILONGJIANG / CHINA" and a simplified city info module or map index-style layout.
[Text System]
The top main title is clear and eye-catching. Keep only 2–4 clear readable texts in the image (e.g., "Yichun Station," "Forestry Road," "Bus Stop"), with other signs using abstracted Chinese strokes or symbolic characters to avoid gibberish. The overall text system should be unified, restrained, and integrated into the design structure.
[Visual Style]
The overall style uses monochrome or a very limited two-color line drawing on an off-white or warm white paper texture. Lines are precise, clean, and layered, with the feel of an architectural pen manuscript and screen print texture. Foreground lines are slightly thicker, background lines thinner, with figures and objects being simple but recognizable. The style is restrained and sophisticated.

[Innovation Requirements]
Incorporate design variations in: title naming system, top layout structure, slightly low-angle street perspective, fusion composition of forest and city, bottom archive-style information module, and expression of autumn fallen leaf atmosphere.
[Avoidance Matters]
No ordinary travel poster style, no watercolor, impasto, or cartoon/chibi styles, no cyberpunk, no high-saturation colors, and no cheap souvenir style.

Please generate an independent, complete, high-definition, clear-lined vertical urban line-drawing poster with a sense of collectability and series identity.
title: Urban Line Atlas Poster
description: A sophisticated prompt for creating high-quality architectural line art posters of specific cities with a vintage handbook aesthetic.

content:
Using REFERENCE_0 as the presenter photo, create a soft, elegant Japanese online seminar flyer in a warm beige/pink watercolor stationery style. Keep the presenter’s hair, outfit, earrings, and anonymized face block consistent, but crop her into the left side of the flyer and extend/generate her upper body so she is smiling toward the viewer with one arm raised and pointing to the event information on the right. Add delicate botanical line art, pale washi-tape textures, thin decorative borders, dotted accents, and pastel blush/gold highlights on an off-white background.

Design the flyer as a vertical event announcement with these discrete content areas: 1 large top headline area, 1 right-side event info card, 1 event theme strip, 1 host/organizer strip, and 3 bottom information cards.

Visible text to include:
Top headline: “ChatGPT Images 2.0で” and large main title “AI画像生成を楽しく学ぼう！”
Near the presenter: “ChatGPT Images 2.0でAI画像生成を極めよう”, “初心者さん向け・やさしく解説”, “AI画像生成が気になる人へ。手を動かしながら、基本と楽しさを学べるオンラインイベント。” and a speech bubble “私がナビゲートします♪ 一緒に楽しく学びましょう！”
Right event info card labeled “EVENT INFO” with “POINT 01”, date/time “5/9（土）22:00〜”, “オンライン開催”, “リアルタイム参加無料”, plus a small speech bubble “夜の時間に、おうちで気軽に参加できます♪”
Event theme strip labeled “EVENT THEME”: “ChatGPT Images 2.0を使った やさしいAI画像生成入門”
Host strip: left circle “主催・登壇”, center “かなまる × おじぎねこ”, subtext “初心者向けにChatGPT Images 2.0での AI画像生成の使い方をやさしく解説”, right speech bubble “初めての方でも安心！やさしく丁寧にお伝えしますね。”
Bottom 3 cards: Card 1 labeled “FOR BEGINNERS / POINT 02 / こんな方に” with 3 checklist items “AI画像生成が気になる人”, “初心者”, “発信素材を作りたい人” and bubble “ひとつでも当てはまればOK!”; Card 2 labeled “POINT / POINT 03 / 参加するとわかること” with 3 bullet items “ChatGPT Images 2.0の基本”, “画像生成の楽しさ”, “発信素材への活用イメージ” and bubble “明日から使えるヒントが見つかります♪”; Card 3 labeled “ENTRY / POINT 04 / 申込方法” with “Peatixイベントページから申し込み” and bubble “お申し込みはとっても簡単です♪”
Footer notes: “※参加URL・アーカイブ・特典条件はPeatixで要確認”, “※事前予約特典あり”, and final call to action “詳細・お申し込みはPeatixへ”.

Make the typography refined and airy, combining elegant Japanese Mincho-style serif text with a handwritten script for “ChatGPT Images 2.0”. Use {argument name="event date and time" default="5/9（土）22:00〜"}, {argument name="main headline" default="AI画像生成を楽しく学ぼう！"}, {argument name="presenter names" default="かなまる × おじぎねこ"}, {argument name="event platform" default="Peatix"}, and {argument name="theme text" default="ChatGPT Images 2.0を使った やさしいAI画像生成入門"}.
